title: WEB DEVELOPER - WordPress/Elementor
description:
WordPress / Elementor Developer — Freelance 



We’re looking for an experienced WordPress/Elementor developer to build two websites from provided design files. This is a freelance, remote role billed hourly. Candidates based anywhere are welcome, but you must be available for at least a few hours overlap with UK business hours (GMT/BST) for communication and collaboration. 

 

About the project 

The Shala yoga studio and The Shala School of Yoga were once under the same directorship and shared a single website (
). They are now two separate businesses, each with their own director. The shared site needs to be separated into two distinct sites: 

— 
 — for Vahni at The Shala  for  yoga studio classes and workshops 

— The Shala School of Yoga under theshalalondon address, for teacher trainings  

 

The project will be divided into two phases as follows and you will bill the two businesses respectively  

 

PHASE 1 – VAHNI  

What you’ll be doing 

— Archiving the existing site properly so all data is retained and retrievable  

 

— Building a WordPress/Elementor Pro site from provided design files  (Figma/HTML mockups)

 

— Working closely with our team on a clear design handoff and building faithfully to that spec 

 

— Migrating and organising content from the existing shared WordPress site into the new site 

 

— Integrating Momence as the booking platform in the same way it is integrated currently 

 

— Setting up a dynamic events/workshop system with SEO-optimised content in WordPress (not reliant on Momence for indexing) 

 

— Implementing SEO migration — preserving existing URLs, 301 redirects, and applying meta data provided by our SEO team 

 

— Ensuring strong UX throughout — the sites must be clean, fast, accessible, and easy to navigate 

 

PHASE 2 – THE SHALA SCHOOL BUILD 

What you’ll be doing 

— Building a WordPress/Elementor Pro site from provided HTML templates 

 

— Working closely with the team on any updates/design tweaks 

 

— Migrating and organising content from the existing WordPress site into the new site 

 

— Integrating Momence as the booking platform – the integration here is very straightforward. 

 

— Implementing SEO migration — preserving existing URLs, 301 redirects, and applying meta data provided by our SEO team 

 

— Ensuring strong UX throughout — the site must be clean, fast, accessible, and easy to navigate  

 

 

What we’re looking for: 

— Strong WordPress and Elementor Pro experience — you must be proficient, not just familiar. Minimum 5 years experience 

 

— Solid knowledge of WordPress plugins — you should know which tools to use and why 

 

— Comfortable building from a designer’s Figma handoff and/or HTML mockups — your role is to build to spec, not interpret design direction 

 

— Experience with booking platform integrations - we work with Momence

 

— Understanding of SEO best practices in WordPress builds 

 

— Strong UX sensibility — you build sites that feel considered, not just functional 

 

— Clear communicator & excellent English — we’ll be working remotely and need daily progress updates 

 

— Available for at least a few hours overlap with UK business hours 

 

Nice to have 

— Familiarity with The Events Calendar or similar WordPress events plugins 

 

— Experience with WPX hosting 

 

To apply 

Please share  

- a link to your portfolio 

- two or three examples of the best WordPress/Elementor sites you’ve built 

- your hourly rate 

- a brief note on your experience with booking system integrations and working from design handoffs would be helpful. 

 

We’re moving quickly on this — responsive candidates will be prioritised. 



*There may be opportunities for ongoing support and maintenance work following launch.

title: Cold Email & GTM Engineer
description:
About GenFlows

GenFlows is a GTM engineering and cold outbound agency based in the Netherlands. We build transparent, client-owned outbound systems using Clay, cold
 infrastructure, and LinkedIn automation. Our partners span B2B verticals including MCA/lending, SaaS, fintech, M&A advisory, and professional services. We operate on a Glass Box philosophy: every system we build, the client owns and understands.

The Role

You will own end-to-end outbound campaign delivery for a dedicated portfolio of client accounts. This means building Clay enrichment and qualification tables, writing cold
 copy, managing sending infrastructure, and continuously optimizing campaign performance. You work directly with clients and collaborate closely with our Delivery Lead to maintain GenFlows quality standards.

What You Will Do

Build and maintain Clay tables for lead enrichment, ICP classification, and qualification scoring across client accounts

Set up and manage cold
 infrastructure (domains, mailboxes, warmup, DNS, deliverability monitoring)

Write, A/B test, and iterate on cold
 sequences tailored to each client's ICP and value proposition

Execute LinkedIn outbound campaigns where applicable, coordinating messaging across channels

Integrate Clay workflows with client CRMs (HubSpot, Salesforce) via webhooks and native integrations

Monitor campaign metrics (open rates, reply rates, bounce rates, SQLs) and present weekly performance reports to clients

Collaborate with the Delivery Lead on campaign strategy, ICP refinement, and copywriting frameworks

Document all systems and processes in line with the Glass Box methodology so clients retain full ownership

What You Bring

Required:

1+ year hands-on experience with 
 (building tables, using enrichment providers, writing Clay prompts, integrating webhooks)

Proven track record running cold
 campaigns with measurable results (reply rates, meetings booked, SQLs generated)

Working knowledge of
 infrastructure: domain setup, SPF/DKIM/DMARC, warmup tools, inbox rotation, and deliverability best practices

Strong copywriting skills for B2B cold outreach across different verticals and buyer personas

Experience with at least one CRM (HubSpot preferred) and basic understanding of data flows between systems

Self-directed work style with clear communication; comfortable working async in a remote team

Nice to Have:

Experience with LinkedIn outbound tools (Expandi, Dripify, or similar)

Familiarity with Instantly, Smartlead, or other cold
 sending platforms

Understanding of B2B verticals like MCA/lending, SaaS, or financial services

Basic API knowledge (REST, webhooks, JSON) for building integrations

Experience working in an agency setting managing multiple client accounts simultaneously

How We Work

Small, focused team: you are not a number. Your work has direct, visible impact on client results and agency growth.

Weekly cadences with the Delivery Lead for campaign reviews, strategy alignment, and knowledge sharing.

Full autonomy within your client accounts. We trust you to make decisions and flag issues proactively.

Glass Box methodology: we build systems that are transparent and documented. No black boxes, no lock-in.

Tools of the trade: Clay, Instantly/Smartlead, HubSpot, LinkedIn, Slack, Notion.

How to Apply

Send a short video intro and your relevant experience with your application. Apply here: 
. 



Optional: Include a brief description of a Clay table or cold
 campaign you built and the results it achieved. No formal CV required -- we care about what you have built and how you think.

title: Hotel and Short Term Rental Revenue Manager (Virtual Assistant)
description:
Role Overview:

Big Easy Management is seeking a highly analytical Revenue Manager (Virtual Assistant) to support pricing, market analysis, and performance optimization across our portfolio of short-term rentals and boutique hotel properties.

This role is responsible for digging into data, identifying opportunities, and helping drive revenue performance at the unit level. The right candidate is proactive, detail-oriented, and constantly looking for ways to improve pricing, positioning, and listing performance.

This is not a reporting-only role. This is also an analysis and opportunity identification role.

*It is important to review the Application Instructions at the end of this document.



Key Responsibilities:



Performance Monitoring:

- Track and monitor past, current, and forward-looking performance of individual properties

- Analyze occupancy, ADR, revenue trends, and booking pace

- Identify underperforming units and recommend specific actions



Comp Set Creation + Competitive Analysis:

- Build and maintain comp sets for each property

- Analyze competitor pricing, listings, amenities, and positioning

- Identify competitive advantages that can be replicated or improved upon



Listing + Revenue Optimization:

- Identify ways to improve listing performance (titles, descriptions, photos, amenities) based on analysis of properties that outperform in their compset

- Recommend pricing adjustments based on demand, seasonality, comp sets, and market trends

- Ensure listings are competitively priced within their market

- Identify opportunities to improve unit performance through amenity upgrades or adjustments based on competitor analysis & guest expectations/feedback

- Track impact of improvements on performance



Promotions + Demand Generation:

- Assist in creating and managing promotional strategies and discount codes for OTAs & direct bookings

- Identify opportunities to drive occupancy during low-demand periods



Market + Neighborhood Analysis:

- Monitor what’s happening in the neighborhoods where our properties operate

- Track events, demand drivers, and seasonality shifts

- Provide insights that inform pricing and positioning decisions



Reporting + Opportunity Tracking:

- Create and maintain reporting that tracks:

- Performance trends

- Changes made and their impact



Market Comparisons:

- Clearly highlight opportunities, risks, and recommended actions

- Focus on actionable insights, not just data





Qualifications:

- This is not an entry-level generic VA role

- Experience in revenue management for hotels and/or short-term rentals (required)

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

- Ability to translate data into clear recommendations

- Experience with pricing tools (Wheelhouse, PriceLabs, or similar preferred)

- Familiarity with Airbnb and property management systems (Hostaway preferred)

- Strong attention to detail and organization

- Applicants must complete the required steps to be considered

- Incomplete applications are not reviewed



Bonus:

Knowledge of and/or experience in the New Orleans market



Reporting Structure:

- Reports directly to the Director of Product

- Works closely with internal teams to support performance improvements



What Success Looks Like:

- Consistent identification of revenue and performance opportunities

- Improved performance across individual units and comp sets

- Clear, actionable reporting that drives decisions

- Proactive recommendations, not reactive reporting



Application Instructions:

 To be considered for this role, all applicants must complete the Criteria Corp assessment and upload a single PDF during the application process. Link to Criteria Corp Here- 

1. Your PDF must include:

2. Your resume

3. Written answers to the questions below

4. Your response to the short exercise



Please begin your document with the phrase: Revenue is in the details.

Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered.



Application Questions:

1. What revenue management experience do you have in hotels and/or short-term rentals?

2. What tools have you used? Please include any experience with Airbnb, Hostaway, Wheelhouse, PriceLabs, AirDNA, Google Sheets, Slack, Gmail, or similar platforms.

3. Tell us about one pricing, listing, amenity, or promotional opportunity you identified that improved performance. What did you find, and what action was taken?

4. What hourly pay are you seeking, what is your weekly availability, and are you willing to work weekends if needed?



Short Exercise:

Please answer the following prompt in 5 to 10 bullet points within the same PDF document:

You are reviewing a 3-bedroom short-term rental with lower-than-expected occupancy over the next 30 days. Nearby comparable listings appear to have stronger reviews, better amenities, and better listing presentation. What would you review first, and what actions would you recommend?

title: Graphic Designer (AU)
description:
Graphic Designer – Exhibitions, Events and Brand Activations



About the Role

We are looking for a talented and highly detail-oriented Graphic Designer to support the creative development and

delivery of exhibitions, events and brand activation projects.



This role is ideal for someone who can take a concept or creative direction and turn it into polished, on-brand design

outcomes, while also preparing accurate production-ready artwork based on dimensions, technical drawings, mark-ups

and artwork schedules.



The role requires a strong eye for design, excellent technical artwork skills, and a practical understanding of how

graphics are applied across physical environments and live experiences.

Experience in exhibitions, events, signage, environmental graphics or brand activations is highly preferred.



Key Responsibilities

• Create graphic design concepts and visual layouts based on client briefs, references and creative direction

• Roll out approved concepts across multipole assets and formats including graphics, signage, counters, walls,

plinths, digital screens, print collateral and branded materials

• Prepare accurate production-ready artwork files for print, signage and digital outputs

• Put together artwork schedules including dimensions, mark-ups working from technical details and production

specifications

• Apply brand guidelines consistency across a wide range of touchpoints

• Adapt master creative across multiple sizes and formats while maintaining design quality and consistency

• Ensure all files are accurate and ready for issue, including checking spelling, alignment, image quality, scale,

bleed, colour settings and layout consistency

• Maintain organised file structures, version control and clear naming conventions

• Communicate clearly and professionally with internal tea
mbers via Outlook, Teams and project updates

• Work collaborative with creative and production teams to meet deadlines and project needs

• Respond to feedback and revisions efficiently and accurately



Skills and Capabilities

• Strong graphic design fundamentals including typography, layout, composition, hierarchy and brand application

• Ability to translate concepts and creative direction into strong visual design outcomes

• Excellent art working skills and technical file setup

• High attention to detail and accuracy

• Ability to read and interpret artwork schedules, dimensions, technical drawings and production requirements

• Strong understanding of print production requirements including beleed, trim, safe zones, resolution and colour

modes

• Ability to design for both print and digital outputs

• Strong time management and ability to handle multiples tasks and deadlines

• Strong written English communications kills

• Ability to work independently while staying connected to a remote team

• Proactive approach to asking questions and flagging issues early



Software and tools

Essential

• Adobe illustrator

• Adobe InDesign

• Adobe Photoshop

• Adobe Acrobat

• Microsoft Outlook

• Microsoft Teams

• Microsoft Word

• Microsoft Excel

• Microsoft PowerPoint



Preferred / nice to have

• Adobe After Effects

• Adobe Premier Pro

• Figma

• Basic understanding of spatial or 3D design tools such as SketchUp

• Experience using shared devices, cloud-based file systems and structure folder management



Experience Required

• Minimum 3 years’ experience in graphic design, artwork production or a similar role

• Strong portfolio demonstrating both creative design and technical artwork capability

• Experience preparing files for print and production

• Experience working in a fast-paced studio, agency or project-based environment

• Experience in exhibitions, events, signage, retail environments, environmental graphics or brand activations is

highly preferred

• Experience working remotely with international teams is an advantage



Personal attributes

• Detail-orientated and organised

• Reliable and responsive

• Calm under pressure and able to manage deadlines

• Collaborative and easy to work with

• Positive attitude and willingness to learn

• Accountable for quality and accuracy

• Able to balance creativity with practical execution



What success looks like in a role

• Creative work is on-brand, well considered and visually strong

• Artwork files are accurate, organised and production-ready

• Deadlines are met and communication is clear

• Technical details are followed carefully reducing production issues and rework

• Design quality remains high across multiple formats and rollout

title: Hiring 4 Tax Accountants (AU)
description:
We believe our people are the foundation of our success. Our culture is built on respect, collaboration, and supporting one another.



We strive to create a workplace where employees feel valued, heard, and empowered to grow both personally and professionally. Our leadership is committed to fostering open communication, professional development, and a positive environment where everyone can perform at their best.



If you're looking for a company that values your contribution and supports your growth, we’d love to have you on our team.



Why Join Bravo?

We believe great people deserve great support. Here’s what you can expect when you join us:

-Competitive and stable full-time work from home opportunity

-HMO and Life Insurance coverage

-13th Month Pay

-Government Contributions (SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG)

-Sick Leave and Vacation Leave benefits

-Free Training and Professional Development opportunities

-Monthly Virtual Employee Engagement activities (with prizes!)

-Fun Company Get-Togethers

-An Annual Christmas Party that our team truly looks forward to

-Year-End Package



We make sure our team not only works hard, but also feels appreciated and celebrated.



About the Role

We are currently looking for four (4) experienced AU Tax Accountants to join our growing team.

In this role, you will work closely with an Australian firm handling tax compliance and accounting services for a diverse client base. This position offers the opportunity to further develop your expertise in Australian taxation while working in a supportive and collaborative environment.



Key Responsibilities

-Prepare and review Australian Tax Returns, BAS, and Financial Statements

-Ensure compliance with Australian tax regulations and legislation

-Provide tax planning support or advice to clients or internal stakeholders

-Analyze financial statements and provide insights where needed

-Maintain accurate financial records and documentation



What We’re Looking For

-2–5+ years of Australian Tax and Business Services experience

-Ability to prepare tax returns for Individuals, Partnerships, Companies (including Group Companies), and Trusts

-Experience in preparing and reviewing Financial Statements

-Solid understanding of Australian tax legislation, including:

Capital Gains Tax (CGT)

Fringe Benefits Tax (FBT)

Division 7A compliance and application

-Experience in preparing IAS and BAS

-Experience in tax planning is an advantage

-Strong analytical and communication skills

-Proficient in using Xero, XPM, and other accounting software

-Positive and proactive attitude with a fun team spirit

-Bookkeeping experience is a plus



We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Remote Productivity Assistant/Coach (Part-Time)
description:
# Remote Productivity Assistant (Part-Time)



**$2/hour | 3 hours/day | Tuesday–Friday (USA)**



---



I'm an independent consultant based in the US. I run multiple projects from home and I'm looking for a remote productivity assistant to help me stay focused and track my morning work block.



This role is part accountability, part monitoring. You'll monitor my work sessions via video call and workspace cameras, log what you observe, check in with me regularly, and be available when I need to talk something through. If I'm not on the call at the start of the shift, you reach out and get things going.



The only deliverable is a simple log you fill out every 5–10 minutes. The rest is showing up, paying attention, and keeping the energy going.



**What you'll do:**

- Monitor my work session via Google Meet, WhatsApp, or workspace cameras depending on the situation

- Check in with me regularly throughout the shift

- Log what you observe using a simple form I'll provide

- Be available to talk — sometimes I need to think out loud or just have someone there

- If I'm not on the call when the shift starts, you reach out until I am



**Good fit if you:**

- Take initiative — you don't wait around, you check in

- Bring energy and encouragement. Think coach energy.

- Are outgoing and comfortable talking to people

- Can be direct when something needs to be said

- Want to practice conversational English — I'm very comfortable with ESL speakers

- Are reliable and show up on time every day

- Can handle a mix of conversation and quiet



**Tech requirements:**

- Stable internet 

- A laptop or desktop with at least 4GB RAM that can run a video call and a camera app at the same time

- A webcam and mic - built-in is fine

- **A space where you can talk freely from 1am–4am PHT**



**Not a VA role.** No admin, no research, no scheduling.



**Schedule:** Tuesday–Friday, 9am–12pm Pacific (1am–4am PHT)

**Pay:** $2/hour

**Duration:** Ongoing



---



**To apply:** Fill out the application form here: 



---



**Tags:** body doubling, ADHD, productivity, accountability partner, cheerleader, motivational support, morning accountability, customer service, English conversation practice, ESL, remote coworking, life coaching, focus partner, time tracking, work from home, graveyard shift, night shift, positive attitude, energy, enthusiasm, video monitoring, CCTV monitoring, remote monitoring

title: Technical Meta Ads & GHL Specialist (Financial Services Clients)
description:
The Role:



We are a fast-paced marketing agency helping wealth management companies scale their businesses. We are looking for a highly technical, experienced Meta Ads and Workflow Automation Specialist to take ownership of our client onboarding, technical implementation, and campaign management.



How we divide the work: You will work directly with myself (Sam Burrows, Founder of Burrows & Partners). My job is the creative vision and growing the agency. Your job is looking after client performance, fixing problems, and handling the flawless technical implementation of our systems.



While we have a creative team and proven templates, creative ability is always a bonus in today's Meta landscape. However, your core superpower must be technical problem-solving. If you love diving into data, troubleshooting broken funnels, setting up seamless automations, and dealing with the tech headaches that I don't enjoy, this is the perfect role for you.



We are looking for someone who thrives on efficiency, speed, and accuracy, and who wants to work for a loyal, supportive, but demanding boss who heavily rewards top performers.



What You Will Do:



•

Client Onboarding & Funnel Architecture: A huge part of this role is onboarding new clients. You will build landing pages in GoHighLevel (GHL) following our SOPs, set up
 sequences, and map out CRM workflows.



•

Automation Implementation: Connect the dots flawlessly. You will set up complex automations using Zapier, webhooks, and native integrations to ensure leads flow perfectly from ad to CRM.



•

Meta Ads Management & Troubleshooting: Launch, manage, and optimise Meta ad campaigns. When tracking breaks or a CPA spikes, you are the first line of defence to diagnose and fix the issue.



•

Navigating Meta Policy & Restrictions: You will be dealing with Meta Business Manager daily. We operate in a regulated industry (Financial Services). You must have deep experience dealing with Meta's advertising policies, navigating account restrictions, and communicating effectively with Meta Marketing Pros. Ideally, you know the Meta policy landscape better than I do.



Who You Are:



•

A Meta Veteran (3+ Years Required): You have a minimum of 3 years of hands-on Meta Ads experience. You have dealt with Meta Marketing Pros, handled account restrictions, and understand the importance of Business Manager health. Experience operating within Meta's financial services advertising policies and Special Ad Categories is preferred.



•

A Technical Troubleshooter: You genuinely enjoy fixing broken systems. You can build multi-step workflows in GoHighLevel and Zapier in your sleep.



•

Creative-Aware: You understand that creative is the biggest driver of Meta ad performance right now. While you are a technical implementer first, any creative copywriting or ad design ability is a bonus.



•

Fast & Efficient: You take a brief, ask the right questions, and implement it quickly and accurately. Occasional weekend monitoring may be required, but you will be compensated and looked after very well for your dedication.



•

Compliant: You understand that working in our environment means adhering to strict operational security (e.g., using dedicated devices for BM access, no VPNs while accessing Meta).



Requirements:



•

Minimum 3 years of proven experience managing and scaling Meta Ads.



•

Deep technical expertise in GoHighLevel (GHL), CRM workflows, and automation (Zapier/Webhooks).



•

Proven experience dealing with Meta account restrictions, policy flags, and Meta support.



•

Bonus: Creative ability (copywriting, understanding what makes a winning ad creative).



•

Bonus: Meta Certified Media Buying Professional status.



•

Bonus: Previous experience running ads in the Financial Services vertical.



How to Apply:



We only hire people with exceptional attention to detail. Do not send a generic cover letter; it will be deleted.



To apply, please fill out our application form: 





And then submit on here with your CV.



We look forward to meeting you!

title: Admin & Operations Assistant
description:
Job Overview:

We are seeking a sharp, resourceful Administrative Assistant to handle the backbone of our daily operations. We aren't looking for a "years of experience" resume; we are looking for raw intelligence and high organizational ability. If you are a fast learner who takes pride in accuracy and order, we want to hear from you.



Your Daily Mission:



Financial Admin: Generating invoices and ensuring all billing data is accurate.



Contract Management: Updating and maintaining client contracts.



Communications: Managing our inbox and responding to
 messages with speed and clarity.



Data Integrity: Keeping Google Spreadsheets and Excel files updated with new orders.



File Management: Maintaining a strict organizational structure for all company files.



Visuals: Basic Photoshop tasks to support document and file presentation.



Who You Are:



Intelligent: You can connect the dots and solve problems without being micro-managed.



Disciplined: You are comfortable working Full-Time on Israel Standard Time (IST).



Technical: Proficient in Google Suite and Microsoft Office. Photoshop skills are a major plus.



Meticulous: You hate mistakes in spreadsheets and invoices.



Salary & Benefits:



Rate: $500 USD per month (Fixed Full-Time).



Training: We will provide the workflow; you provide the brainpower.



Stability: Long-term position with a straightforward, no-nonsense team.



How to Apply:

We filter for attention to detail immediately. To be considered, your subject line must be: "Operations Assistant - [Your Name] - [Your Favorite Animal]".



In your application, please answer the following:



Confirm you are available for full-time work during Israel business hours.



What is your process for double-checking your work for errors?



What is the most technical thing you have done in Excel or Google Sheets?

title: Lead Generation Specialist – Gaming / Forex (Remote)
description:
Remote Lead Generation Specialist – Gaming / Forex Industry



Remote | Flexible hours



We are a fintech payment company working with online businesses in Southeast Asia.



Our clients include:



• Online gaming platforms

• Forex & trading companies

• Digital platforms with high payment volume



We are currently looking for people who enjoy researching websites, finding business contacts, and starting conversations with potential partners.



What You Will Do



Your main job is to help us find potential merchants and start the first conversation.



Typical tasks include:



• Research gaming or trading websites

• Identify websites with active deposit/payment systems

• Find
, WhatsApp, or
 contact of the operator

• Send a short introduction message about our payment service

• Introduce interested merchants to our team for further discussion



Most operators in this industry communicate through
, so the ability to locate and contact merchants on
 is important.



What Happens After That

rchants you introduce

Work Schedule

• Full-time

Ideal Candidate

, WhatsApp, or
 outreach

Application Questions

Are you comfortable completing a short research task within 48 hours?



Applications without answers may not be reviewed.

title: Remote Land Sourcing & Research Specialist
description:
REMOTE LAND SOURCING & RESEARCH SPECIALIST (UK PROPERTY DEVELOPMENT)

We are a UK-based property development and investment company focused on identifying land and development opportunities. We are looking for a reliable and experienced Land Sourcing Specialist from the Philippines for a long-term, full-time role.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Source land and development opportunities using tools like LandTech and Searchland

Research planning permissions, local plans, and site constraints

Identify off-market opportunities with development potential

Analyse ownership details and planning viability

Maintain a pipeline of high-quality sites

Monitor UK council planning portals

Prepare simple reports on potential deals



REQUIREMENTS:

Experience using LandTech, Searchland, or similar software (ESSENTIAL)

Understanding of the UK planning system (preferred)

Experience in property, land sourcing, or real estate research

Strong written and spoken English

Detail-oriented and proactive

Reliable internet (minimum 25 Mbps)

Quiet workspace with laptop and headset



COMPENSATION:

Base Salary: $600 per month



Performance Bonus: $1,000 per successful deal sourced

Bonus applies when a deal you identify is taken forward by the company.

This role is ideal for someone motivated, driven, and looking to increase earnings through performance.



WORK SCHEDULE:

Full-Time

Monday to Friday

HOW TO APPLY:

Your CV

"I am your next Land Sourcing Specialist."

title: Sales Associate Manager (Analyst) – Ticketing
description:
Company Description



Our company specializes in developing innovative solutions within the live entertainment industry, focusing on enhancing fan experiences and engagement. By creating B2B and B2C systems, the company aims to provide tools and services that empower promoters, marketplaces, artists, and marketers to better serve their audiences. The company also explores cross-border inventory and content distribution opportunities while pioneering e-commerce concepts for underserved fans. Driven by innovation, the company is passionate about advancing the live entertainment ecosystem.

This roles sits within the Ticket Distribution/ Broker business.



Role Description



We are hiring a Sales Associate Manager to support the Sales Manager at a fast-growing ticketing business focused on distributing concerts, sports and other major live events across the UK, Europe and Asia.



This is a junior commercial role within the Sales team. The person will support on inventory acquisition, category management, and event analysis, while developing into a future Sales Manager with stronger ownership across pricing, category expertise, and new event identification.



We are looking for someone who is smart, highly analytical, detail-oriented, and eager to learn quickly. You must already have at least 1 year of experience in ticketing





Main responsibilities



Acquire inventory and/or coordinate inventory acquisition across key events

Support ticket category management

Help build ownership over one category, vertical or smaller market

Research events, supply/demand patterns, pricing and market behaviour

Support commercial analysis and business cases

Work closely with the Sales Manager on execution and opportunity sizing

Maintain trackers and keep commercial activity organised





Requirements



At least 1 year of experience in secondary market ticketing

Good understanding of inventory acquisition, management and pricing

Strong analytical mindset

Very comfortable with Excel/ Data visualisation tools

Strong attention to detail and reliability

Fast learner with good commercial judgment

Experience with Viagogo, StubHub, Skybox or similar tools is a strong plus

Background in Mathematics or another quantitative field is a big plus





This is a great role for someone who wants to build a long-term commercial career in ticketing and live entertainment, and grow into a broader Sales Manager role over time.



What we offer



Competitive package with a significant variable component tied to commercial results (2x OTE).

Opportunity to work and get mentored by the Directors and be a core part of the company’s hyper-growth story having the support form the rest of the team.

Remote-first setup with flexibility, while following Europe (GMT) working hours.

Best in industry benefits: HMO, remote work allowance, PTO, paid coworking, learning & development budget, 1x event experience per year.

A builder culture: space to experiment, propose ideas and shape how we make commercial decisions.

Exposure to multiple markets (UK, Europe, Asia, America) and categories (music, sports, premium events).

title: Full-Stack Software Developer (Automation, Ticketing)
description:
Company Description



The company specializes in building innovative ventures and systems within the live entertainment industry to better serve fans. We build technology-driven businesses that improve how fans discover, access and engage with live experiences globally.



Our work focuses on four strategic pillars:

E-commerce Experiences

B2B Digital Infrastructure

Global Ticket Distribution

Next-Generation Fan Engagement



This role will initially sit within our Ticket Distribution business, where we are building proprietary systems and automation infrastructure to support a rapidly growing operation.





Role Description



We are hiring a Full-Stack Software Developer to help scale the technology behind our operating model.

You will work directly with the Managing Director to build internal tools, integrate complex APIs, automate workflows and develop new consumer-facing platforms. This is a high-ownership role suited for someone who enjoys building systems end-to-end and solving complex technical challenges.





Key Responsibilities



Build and maintain internal tools and oversee the development of our core portal

Integrate and manage complex third-party APIs across marketplaces, partners and platforms

Design automation systems using n8n, Node.js and Python to improve operational workflows

Apply scraping, automation and AI agents across internal tools and processes

Expand data capture and analytics capabilities to improve operational insights

Build and oversee consumer-facing ecommerce platforms and integrations

Implement fingerprinting and profiling systems to support commerce infrastructure

Develop integrations with external partners, including travel industry APIs

Ensure clean architecture, maintainable code and scalable systems





Requirements



3–5+ years of professional software development experience

Must provide examples of past projects and at least one production web application you have built

Experience in ecommerce, sneakers or ticketing ecosystems

Strong system design and problem-solving skills

Ability to work independently in a remote environment

Strong communication and collaboration skills





Technical Requirements



Proficiency in Full-Stack Development, Software Development, and a deep understanding of both front-end and back-end workflows. 

Strong experience with Next.js (full-stack), React and TypeScript

Experience with Node.js and Python

Experience building and integrating REST APIs

Familiarity with Supabase and PostgreSQL or similar databases

Experience with workflow automation tools such as n8n

Knowledge of Cloudflare, VPS management and Linux environments

Experience with version control and code review practices

Ability to design scalable and maintainable software architecture





What We Offer



A builders-first environment with high ownership and impact on real products, alongside best-in-industry remote benefits including learning and development budgets, work-from-home allowances, health insurance support, phantom equity participation opportunities, structured career growth frameworks, and many more



Next Steps



Apply for the role by submitting your CV and examples of previous projects.

We will schedule an initial interview to discuss your background and experience.

If successful, you will have a simple technical interview.

Selected candidate will complete a 3–4 week project to evaluate collaboration and technical fit.

After successful completion, you will be invited to join the company full-time.

title: Video Editor Content Creator
description:
We are an e-commerce company based in California, USA. We've been in business for over ten years and have extensive experience in eCommerce. We're currently looking for a few high-energy, good communicators, punctual, able to follow instructions, analytical, and hard workers.



The ideal candidates must be fluent in English (both spoken and written) and have prior remote working experience. The candidates should be available to work 40 hours per week, have access to a fast and stable internet connection, and own a functioning web camera and microphone for daily video calls. The weekly salary is $200 per week via PayPal.



To skip the interview process and be considered for the position, please upload a video of yourself answering the questions below honestly and in detail, and provide the link. If we believe you’re a good fit for the role, we’ll send you a job offer within a few days.



1. What type of work experience do you have?



2. What is your dream job?



3. What is your favorite movie?



4. Do you think a University/College education is essential?



5. Are you currently employed, and when can you start working?



Thank you for your interest, and see you on the other side.

title: Content Operations VA
description:
The role

Around Finance is a modern Irish accountancy firm. Our founder Richie Lennon creates all our content — blogs, LinkedIn posts, videos, and a monthly newsletter. Your job is to take that content and get it live. Formatted, scheduled, and published properly across WordPress, social media, and HubSpot.



Right now this is primarily an execution role. Richie writes and films, you make it happen. But if you have good content instincts and want to contribute ideas over time that door is open. We want someone great at the job first who can grow from there.



Once content is approved it is your responsibility to ensure it goes live correctly and on time. You own that layer completely.



What you will do every week

• Review Richie's voice note transcripts in Notion daily — categorise by content pillar and sector, flag the strongest ideas for his Friday session

• Format approved blogs in WordPress — headings, Yoast SEO fields, featured image from ChatGPT, correct categories, tags, and internal links

• Schedule LinkedIn posts across personal profile and company page in Metricool — personal content reposts to company page 24 hours later

• Upload reels and schedule Instagram and
 posts on the agreed weekly calendar

• Keep WordPress ticking — plugin updates, broken link checks, minor page edits when briefed

• Send a short weekly summary every Thursday — what is live, what is scheduled, anything flagged

• Prepare a Friday review pack — all content ready for Richie's approval before anything goes live



Once a month:

• Build the monthly
 newsletter in HubSpot — pull from the month's blogs and content, format for send, proof it, and submit for Richie's approval before it goes out



Tasks are assigned in batches and expected to be completed within 24 to 48 hours. The content calendar needs to stay 7 days ahead at all times.



Tools

The main tools are Notion, WordPress, Yoast SEO, Metricool, HubSpot, ChatGPT, Canva, and Google Drive. If you haven't used some of these specifically that's fine — experience in Buffer, Hootsuite, Mailchimp, Airtable, or similar tools means you'll pick up the specifics quickly. HubSpot experience is a bonus — willingness to learn it fast is a must.



What we're looking for

• At least 2 years as a content VA, social media VA, or digital marketing assistant

• Previous experience with US, Australian, or UK clients — strong preference, not optional

• Background in professional services, marketing agencies, or tech companies is a real advantage — you already understand the pace and the standards

• Excellent written English — you'll be formatting and publishing content under a named professional's brand

• Available and reactive — you respond within working hours, flag problems early, and don't go quiet for days

• Process-driven — you follow a brief cleanly without needing constant direction



We'd like to see examples of your work — a portfolio, a sample, a short Loom walkthrough, or anything that shows us how you work. We're not expecting access to a previous client's private site. Just show us what you can do.



This role lives or dies on consistency under low supervision. If you need to be chased, go quiet for days, or find process-driven work frustrating — this won't work.



What success looks like

• Content is always live on time

• The calendar is always 7 days ahead

• No errors in published content or the newsletter

• Richie never has to follow up on execution

• Weekly summaries are short, clear, and accurate

• When something goes wrong you flag it before he notices — not after



Not a fit

• You're looking for a full-time role — this is around 5 hours a week

• You've only worked for local clients or entry-level accounts

• You go quiet for days or need a lot of direction to get started

• You find process-driven execution work frustrating



Paid trial task

Before any offer we ask shortlisted candidates to complete a paid two-hour trial. You'll format one blog post in a WordPress test environment with Yoast fields, a ChatGPT image, and internal links. You'll also categorise three voice note transcripts in a Notion template we provide.



Paid at the agreed hourly rate. We're looking for accuracy and the ability to follow a brief without a lot of back and forth.



How to apply

To apply, submit your application through this platform. Start your message with the word FOUNDERS — applications that don't open with this word won't be read

 Then tell us who you've worked for (especially US, AUS, or UK clients), share examples of your work, list your tools and experience level, and tell us your availability and rate.

title: Property Account Manager
description:
Job Title: Utilities Setup and Accounts Clean-Up Coordinator

Job Type: Fixed-term contract

Contract Length: 4 weeks fixed

Working Hours: Monday to Friday, aligned to UK business hours



Contract Fee: PH Peso 40,000 fixed for the 4-week contract

Bonus: PH Peso 10,000 completion bonus based on successful delivery of agreed milestones, weekly handovers, and final project completion

Start Date: Immediate





Job Description

We are a UK property business managing a shared housing portfolio across multiple houses. We are looking for one highly organised and reliable coordinator based in the Philippines to take ownership of a short-term but important utilities and account clean-up project.



The purpose of this role is to review, organise, set up, and clean up utility-related accounts across up to 21 houses. This includes electricity, gas, water, internet, and council tax-related coordination where required.



This is not a long-term admin role. This is a focused 4-week contract for someone who can come in, sort out the utility structure properly, clean up old issues, contact suppliers, help put payment systems in place, research better rates, and leave behind a clean and usable operating system for the business.



You will be expected to work through active and old account issues, chase suppliers properly, set up direct debits, help organise payment plans for debts where needed, and create clear trackers and SOPs for future management.

By the end of the contract, the utility infrastructure should be properly organised, documented, and ready for ongoing use.





What You Will Be Responsible For

You will be responsible for:



Setting up new utility accounts for up to 20 houses, including electricity, gas, water, internet, and council tax-related coordination where required

Reviewing and cleaning up old utility accounts to make sure records are accurate and issues are identified properly and setting up payment plan

Contacting suppliers directly to resolve account issues, confirm balances, update details, and move accounts into the correct structure via calls

Helping arrange payment plans for debts or arrears where needed and ensuring these are recorded clearly

Setting up direct debits and payment arrangements correctly for active accounts

Researching better rates, tariffs, and suppliers where relevant

Negotiating workable arrangements or improved deals with suppliers where possible

Building a clean utility account structure that is organised, trackable, and usable going forward

Preparing clear trackers, notes, and records so the business has full visibility across all utility accounts

Creating a simple SOP on spreadsheet and word doc and final handover so the utility setup can be maintained properly after the contract ends



Project Deliverables

By the end of the 4-week contract, we expect:

A full list of all houses and their utility account status

New accounts opened where required

Old accounts reviewed and cleaned up where possible

Debt positions identified and payment plans arranged where needed

Direct debits set up and recorded

Supplier contact notes and account updates maintained clearly

Better tariff or supplier opportunities flagged where relevant

A clean master utility tracker for future use

A simple SOP showing how utility accounts should be managed going forward

A final handover showing what has been completed, what is pending, and what future management should look like





How the Project Will Be Managed:

This contract will be managed in weekly phases with clear handovers.

Each week, you will be expected to provide:

A summary of what has been completed

A list of what remains open

Any blockers or supplier delays

Notes on what action has been taken

Priorities for the following week

The project is expected to be completed properly within the 4-week contract period.





Who This Role Is For:

This role is for someone who is highly organised, good at follow-up, comfortable dealing with suppliers, and able to bring structure to messy account situations.

You should be confident handling multiple houses, multiple accounts, and multiple supplier conversations at the same time.

You should be able to work independently, keep records properly, and push issues forward without constant supervision.

This role suits someone who is practical, detail-focused, calm under pressure, and good at getting admin and supplier issues resolved properly.





Essential Requirements:

You must:

Have experience in account setup, supplier coordination, admin clean-up, utilities handling, or similar operational support work

Be comfortable managing multiple properties, accounts, or suppliers at the same time

Have strong written and spoken English

Be highly organised and detail-focused

Be confident following up with suppliers and pushing issues toward resolution

Be comfortable using spreadsheets, trackers, and written process notes

Be available for a fixed 4-week project aligned to UK business hours

Have a stable remote setup with reliable internet and laptop





Preferred Experience:



It is preferred if you have experience in one or more of the following:

UK property or portfolio administration

Utility account coordination

Council tax or supplier handling

Debt follow-up or payment plan setup

Process building or SOP creation

Property operations, facilities admin, or infrastructure coordination



Compensation Structure:

This role is offered on a 4-week fixed contract fee of  PH Peso 40,000.

In addition, there is  PH Peso 10,000 completion bonus available based on successful delivery of agreed milestones, weekly handovers, and final project completion.

The completion bonus is not automatic and will only be paid if the work is completed properly, handed over clearly, and the agreed project scope is delivered to a satisfactory standard.



How to Apply

 your CV to 
 with the subject line:

Utilities Setup and Accounts

Along with your CV, send a short audio / video introduction, answer the following:



Have you handled multiple properties, suppliers, or account issues at the same time

Give one real example of a messy account, supplier, or admin issue you helped resolve

Why do you believe you are suitable for a 4-week project-based role with clear delivery targets

title: Creative Design Assistant & Digital Illustrator
description:
Urgent Hiring — Applications Reviewed on a Rolling Basis. The selection process may take up to 4 weeks, but early applicants are strongly encouraged.



Make Your Mark in Design, Lead Creativity, and Contribute to Our Vision.



Hadley Designs is seeking a Creative Design Assistant & Digital Illustrator to join our creative team and play a pivotal role in crafting high-quality, visually stunning designs that enhance our brand presence. If you are a creative powerhouse proficient in Adobe Illustrator with a keen eye for design, we want you to help bring our vision to life.



This is a hands-on, dynamic role ideal for a creative professional passionate about design and eager to make an impact on a fast-growing company. You’ll collaborate with leadership, work on high-priority assignments, and ensure the seamless execution of design projects that elevate Hadley Designs' brand.



About Hadley Designs

Hadley Designs is a family-founded, female-led brand committed to shaping brighter futures through creativity and purposeful design. We create screen-free learning tools that spark curiosity, build confidence, and transform everyday 
nts into meaningful growth. Each product is hand-drawn, crafted with input from teachers, parents, and child development experts, ensuring learning is joyful, engaging, and accessible.



Our mission is simple: to help families and educators unlock children’s potential with tools that inspire curiosity, strengthen connections, and support learning without screens. Trusted by over two million customers worldwide, we are passionate about designing experiences that make children feel supported, capable, and excited to learn.



If you’re looking for a company that designs with purpose and builds with heart, you’ll feel right at home here.



How You’ll Win at Hadley Designs



We believe in structured creativity. Success in this role means owning your projects and delivering high-quality designs that resonate with our customers while maintaining our visual standards. We’re looking for someone who thrives in a creative environment, is proactive, and possesses a strong attention to detail.



Every Design Assistant is expected to:



Learn the Playbook – Understand our design standards, expectations, and creative processes.

Apply the Playbook – Execute designs consistently, meeting project requirements and adhering to brand guidelines.

Enhance the Playbook – Suggest creative improvements and innovative design strategies after mastering the basic framework.

We value innovation grounded in proven execution and a clear understanding of design principles.



What You’ll Own



-Creation of high-quality, error-free design assets for advertising and marketing campaigns.

-Maintaining consistency in typography, color schemes, and layout across all platforms.

-Regular updates and design of storefront assets for Amazon, Shopify, and other platforms.

-Designing A+ Content templates and custom brand image ads.

-Handling urgent design needs for product launches and special projects.

-Working closely with leadership to ensure deadlines are met and projects are delivered on time.



What Success Looks Like

Within your first 90 days, you will:



-Learn and execute Hadley Designs’ design standards with precision.

-Contribute fresh design ideas that align with the brand and improve our current processes.

-Deliver high-quality artwork that meets or exceeds expectations and is on-brand.

-Support Becca and leadership with urgent creative tasks and maintain consistency in all design work.



Apply Now, Lead Creativity, and Shape Our Future.



Requirements

Who You Are:



-2+ years of professional experience in graphic design or related fields, with a strong portfolio.

-Proficient in Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop, with a strong grasp of CMYK color management and print-ready file preparation.

-Excellent attention to detail and ability to produce high-quality visuals with minimal revisions.

-Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.

-Comfortable receiving and implementing feedback while maintaining a high standard of work.

-Creative, collaborative, and self-driven, motivated by quality and continuous improvement.



Benefits

Why You’ll Thrive Here:



-Join a creative, high-growth company with an established 10+ year track record.

-Own significant projects and make an impact from day one.

-Flexibility to work remotely with a flexible schedule.

-Opportunities for personal development and direct interaction with the Chief Creative Officer.

-A culture of collaboration, trust, and innovation, where your contributions are valued.



Apply Now

If you're a passionate graphic designer who thrives in a dynamic and creative environment, we'd love to hear from you. Apply today to join Hadley Designs and help shape the future of our brand!



P.S. If you don’t hear from us within four weeks of your application, please consider that as our decision to move forward with other candidates, but we truly appreciate your interest in Hadley Designs.



To formally apply : apply here

title: Online English/World History Teachers (Near-Native Accent)
description:
bout Pomelo Academy

Pomelo Academy is an innovative online international school dedicated to delivering high-quality education to students worldwide. We provide a dynamic and engaging learning environment, offering individualized instruction that transcends geographical boundaries.



Job Description

We are seeking passionate and experienced near-native English-speaking online teachers with strong backgrounds in English and/or World History. Candidates may specialize in one subject (English or History) or be able to teach both. The ideal teachers will have experience working with young learners and be committed to creating a supportive and engaging online classroom.



Responsibilities

• Provide instruction in English and/or World History to individuals and small groups of 2–6 students aged 5–18.

• Utilize international-standard curricula.

• Create and deliver engaging lesson plans tailored to each student’s needs.

• Monitor and assess student progress, providing regular feedback to students and parents.

• Foster a positive and encouraging learning environment.



Qualifications

• Professional teacher with at least 2 years of teaching experience (Kindergarten–Grade 12) and verifiable teaching certifications such as TESOL, TEFL, etc.

• Near-native English accent.

• Strong academic background in English and/or World History (additional experience in Science/Economics is a plus).

• Ability to work between 17:00–22:00 (Weekdays) and 09:00–22:00 (Weekends) GMT+8.

• Experience teaching in public/international schools.

• Skilled at working with young learners aged 5–18

• Familiarity with ClassIn and digital literacy (Microsoft Suite and other educational platforms).

• Must have a stable and fast internet connection and a PC/laptop with at least Windows 10/Mac OS Catalina.



Benefits

• Flexible working hours.

• Opportunity to teach using internationally recognized curricula.

• Work with an international team of educators.

• Professional development and training opportunities.

• Regular enrollment system (not booking-based), ensuring a stable and consistent teaching schedule.

title: Phone Videographer
description:
Earn ?12,000/month filming products with your phone at your local mall.



No experience needed. No editing needed. No talking needed. Just your smartphone and a steady hand.



We run a product review channel. We need someone in Manila or Cebu to film short, silent product clips at SM Mall, Robinsons, or any large electronics/home goods store. We handle all editing, voiceover, and post-production — you just film.



PAY:

- Fixed ?12,000/month

- Minimum 300 product videos per month (about 8 mall visits)

- Paid monthly via PayPal

- You choose your own schedule — go whenever it's convenient for you



WHAT YOU'LL DO:



1. Go to any large mall near you

2. Find products on the shelves and film them. All categories are welcome as long as the product sells on Amazon US. That said, some categories earn more for us than others, so when you have a choice, here's the priority:



   TOP TIER: Headphones, earbuds, portable speakers, soundbars, projectors, kitchen appliances (air fryers, blenders, coffee machines)

   STRONG: Home improvement tools, smart home devices, beauty products, office equipment

   GOOD: Toys, pet products, outdoor gear, furniture, home decor, garden tools



   Everything else is still worth filming — if it's on a shelf and it's on Amazon US, film it.



3. Film the price tag or product label clearly and up close (5 sec)

4. Slowly walk around the product — show the build quality, materials, buttons, ports, packaging, and any features or accessories (60-90 sec)

5. Upload the clips to a shared Google Drive folder



IMPORTANT FILMING RULES:



- No talking in the video. Silent footage only.

- Wait until the aisle is clear before filming. No other shoppers should be visible in the shot.

- Keep the camera steady and move slowly. Think "product showcase", not vlog.

- Never film the same product twice. If you've already filmed it, skip it and find something new.

- Before filming, quickly check if the product is sold on Amazon US (I will show you how — it takes 5 seconds with an app on your phone).



WHAT GETS REJECTED (does not count toward your 300):

- Blurry or shaky video

- Product label not clearly readable

- Other people visible in the shot

- Duplicate product (already filmed before)

- Products not sold on Amazon US



I will send you example videos I filmed myself so you can see exactly what a good clip looks like. The bar is not high — just clear, steady, and focused on the product.



This is perfect for students, freelancers, or anyone who visits malls regularly and wants easy extra income on their own schedule.



TO APPLY: Film a quick test video of any product near you right now. Show the label up close, then slowly walk around the product for 30 seconds. No talking. Attach it to your application.

title: Part-time product video filmer - work from your local mall
description: I'm looking for someone in Manila or Cebu to film short product videos inside SM Mall, Robinsons, or any large electronics/home goods store near you. No experience needed. You only need a smartphone with a decent camera. WHAT YOU'LL DO: - Visit a large store 1-2x per week - Film 60-90 second clips of products on the shelf - First shot must be the price tag or product label, clearly readable - Then slowly walk around the product, close-ups of features and accessories - Name each video folder after the product (e.g. "Sony Headphones WH1000XM5") - Upload to Google Drive and share with me PAY: - $0.20 per approved product video, paid weekly via PayPal - Bonus: $2 for every 20 approved products in one session - Blurry, wrong product, or missing label shot = not paid REALISTIC EARNINGS: A 2-3 hour session filming 40 products = $8-10 plus bonuses. You choose when you go. TO APPLY: Send a short test video of any product near you. Film the label clearly, then walk around the product for 30 seconds.

title: E-Commerce Email Designer
description:
This is NOT a drag-and-drop design job.



We need a designer who THINKS about what they're creating. Someone who reads copy and instinctively knows how to restructure it visually so people actually read it. Someone who spots awkward phrasing or AI-sounding bullshit and fixes it. 



Someone who turns walls of text into scannable, engaging layouts that drive clicks.



If you just paste copy into Figma and make it "look nice" - this role isn't for you.

If you actively improve every
 you touch - keep reading.



WHO WE ARE:

We're an
 marketing agency helping premium DTC brands add additional revenue through
. Our
s don't just look good - they look SO GOOD, THEY SELL & MAKE ECOM CUSTOMERS FALL IN LOVE.



THE PROBLEM YOU'LL SOLVE:

Our copywriters write the messaging. But copy alone doesn't convert. YOU transform that copy into visual experiences that:

- Guide the reader's eye exactly where we want it

- Break up text so it's actually readable on mobile

- Create visual rhythm that keeps people scrolling

- Make the CTA impossible to miss



WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE DIFFERENT:

You're not just a designer. You're a visual editor. When you receive copy, you:

- Read it critically (Does this flow? Does this make sense?)

- Restructure it visually (How do I break this into digestible chunks?)

- Flag issues proactively ("This section is confusing - here's how I'd reword it")

- Add visual storytelling (Icons, dividers, imagery that reinforces the message)

- Optimize for scanning (Most people skim - make key points pop)

- FIND what you need to make it great (Missing a product image? You go find it. Need a lifestyle shot? You source it. You don't wait to be spoon-fed every asset - you take initiative to make the
 as good as it can be.)



WHAT YOU'LL DESIGN:

- 25
s per week (5+ per day)

- Campaign
s: Product launches, sales, promotions, content

- Flow
s: Welcome series, abandoned cart, post-purchase, winback

- Style: Premium, high-converting, mobile-first



WHAT "HIGH QUALITY" MEANS TO US:

- Visual hierarchy that guides the eye (not everything screaming for attention)

- White space used strategically (breathing room, not emptiness)

- Text broken into scannable chunks (no walls of copy)

- Pattern interrupts that keep readers engaged (not the same layout repeated)

- CTAs that stand out without being obnoxious

- Mobile-first design (thumb-friendly, fast-loading)

- Brand consistency across every



Examples of the quality we expect: 



WHAT WE DON'T WANT:

- Generic templates with copy pasted in

- Designs that look pretty but ignore readability & conversion

- "Set it and forget it" mentality

- Designers who wait to be told what to fix

- Big blocks of text because "that's what the copy said"



REQUIREMENTS:

- 1+ years designing e-commerce
s (show me
s, not websites)

- Portfolio showing BEFORE/AFTER or explaining your design decisions

- Figma proficiency

- Strong eye for typography and spacing

- Understanding of
 rendering constraints

- Fluent English (you'll be suggesting copy edits)

- Can commit to 9
s weekly, consistently



YOU'LL STAND OUT IF YOU:

- Can articulate WHY you made specific design choices

- Have reworded or restructured copy to improve an

- Understand what makes people click (not just what looks good)

- Notice things others miss (alignment, spacing, flow issues)

- Are opinionated about design (and can back it up)

- Have worked with premium/luxury brands



COMPENSATION:

$400USD /month



WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU:

- Creative ownership (we want your input, not just execution)

- Work with premium brands (portfolio pieces you'll be proud of)

- Consistent weekly volume (predictable income)

- Direct feedback loop (we share what's converting)

- Growth path to Senior Designer / Art Director as we scale



HOW TO APPLY:

1. Send your portfolio 
 designs specifically)

2. Tell me:



   - What's the difference between a good
 design and a great one?

   - Tell me about a time you improved copy or layout beyond what was asked

   - What do most
 designers get wrong?



(You can use AI to answer these questions; however, I want your real, sincere answers, not AI bullshit please)



3. Include 5-8 examples of your best
 work (the more the better)



Serious candidates will complete a test project before hiring:

- We provide: Brand guidelines + product images + brief

- You design: 1 complete

- Timeline: 24 hours



We respond to all applications within 48 hours.





REAL TALK:



I want someone who actually cares about getting better at this. Not just clocking in.



If you're that person, I'll invest in you. Train you properly. Help you build a portfolio that opens doors. The goal is we both grow from this.

title: YouTube Thumbnail Designer (CTR-Focused) – $5 Per Thumbnail
description:
I am looking for a sharp, data-driven Thumbnail Designer who understands that a thumbnail’s only job is to get the click. I don’t just want "nice" designs; I want high-performance visuals that stop the scroll.



This is a gig-based role. Please note: For most videos, I will require at least 2 different thumbnail variations (different concepts/angles), which will be paid at $5 USD each ($10 total for the pair).



Pay: $5 USD per individual thumbnail.

Tools: Photoshop, Canva, or AI-assisted workflows are welcome, provided the final result is high-quality and follows YouTube best practices.



What I am looking for:



CTR Awareness: You understand visual hierarchy and how to use high-contrast colors to stand out on a crowded feed.



Psychology of the Click: You know how to create curiosity or emotional triggers through imagery and minimal text (3 words or less).



Mobile-First Design: Your text must be readable and your focal point must be clear even on a small phone screen.



Efficiency: I am looking for someone who has a fast, reliable workflow and can deliver variations quickly.



Responsibilities:



Create custom thumbnails (usually 2 variations per video) based on provided titles/briefs.



Incorporate provided assets (photos/branding) efficiently.



Standard turnaround: 24–48 hours.



How to Apply:



Send a link to your Portfolio (specifically YouTube thumbnails).



Tell me: What is one technique you use to increase CTR?



Include the word "CLICK" in your subject line so I know you read the full post.

title: Senior Temu Growth Partner – US LLC – High Profit Share
description:
About the Role & Infrastructure

I am Advocate Matan Leker, owner of Leker Projects LLC. We operate a high-tier eCommerce infrastructure:



Verified US LLC with Mercury Bank.



Shopify Collective supply chain with authentic US-based suppliers.



Current Status: Account is live but stuck at 4 impressions.



I am NOT looking for a general VA. I am looking for a Growth Shark who has personally scaled Temu stores (Semi-Managed) to 5 and 6-figure monthly profits.



The Mission

Your sole responsibility is the P&L. You are the driver of this "Ferrari".



Break the Stagnation: Move the store from 4 impressions to high-velocity sales within the first 48-72 hours.



Algorithm Manipulation: Execute the 'Price-Gap Sequence' and 'Velocity Seeding' to trigger the Temu 'New Arrival' boost.



Automation Mastery: Maintain zero-latency inventory and tracking sync between Shopify Collective and Temu using advanced Middleware/API.



Requirements (No Exceptions)

Proven Temu Record: You must have managed at least 2 Temu Semi-Managed stores with over $20,000 monthly profit.



Technical Depth: Deep understanding of Temu's US-to-US shipping compliance and "White-label" requirements.



Operational Dedication: Available for 5-8 hours daily of high-focus management.



Compensation

Base Retainer: $800 - $1,000/month (for the right expert).



Aggressive Profit Share: You will earn a significant percentage of the Net Profit once we cross the $20,000/month milestone.



HOW TO APPLY (The Evidence Test)

Failure to follow these 3 steps will result in immediate disqualification:



Start your application with the words "LEKER SHARK."



The Loom Requirement: Attach a 1-minute Loom video. You MUST refresh the browser while showing a live Temu Seller Center dashboard you have managed. Show the Sales, Account Health, and Shipping Performance.



Technical Answer: Briefly explain your specific technical maneuver to fix 4-impression stagnation for a US-to-US store within 48 hours.



I hire based on admissible evidence and results. If you are an amateur, do not waste my time.



Best regards,

Adv. Matan Leker

Owner, Leker Projects LLC

title: Kajabi Web Designer Expert
description:
Web Developer & Designer Needed to Rebuild The Shala School of Yoga Website on Kajabi 

Project Type: One-off project with potential ongoing hourly work 

Experience Level: Intermediate to Expert 



 

About Us 

The Shala School of Yoga (TSSY) is a well-established UK-based yoga and wellness organisation offering: 

Yoga teacher trainings (200- and 300-hour programs) 

Continuing professional development (CPD) courses 

Yoga Retreats 



We are ready to refresh our online presence by rebuilding our website on Kajabi, moving away from WordPress, to create a clean, modern, and easy-to-manage site that fully reflects our brand and offerings. 



Scope of Work 

We are looking for an experienced Kajabi Web Developer & Designer with strong design sensibility to: 

- Rebuild our existing WordPress site into Kajabi 

- Recommend the best Kajabi templates or customisations for our needs 

- Design a polished, modern, user-friendly site that aligns with TSSY’s brand 

- Build a structure that is easy for our team to update and maintain 

- Integrate required third-party tools, including 
nce plug ins

- Set up clear navigation and intuitive site architecture 

- Preserve and migrate all existing SEO work (page titles, meta descriptions, headings, alt text) 

- Implement 301 redirects for any changed URLs to maintain search engine rankings 

Note: This is not a full-from-scratch design job. We would like to work from Kajabi templates so long as it feels professional and cohesive. 



Required Experience 

- Experience working with Kajabi or similar template-based platforms 

- 3+ years professional web development experience 

- Strong design skills; able to produce clean, modern, stylistically consistent websites 

- Experience with website rebuilds, domain migrations, and third-party integrations 

- Excellent communication skills in English 

- High attention to detail, reliability, and responsiveness 



How to Apply 

To be considered, please include all of the following in your proposal: 

- One portfolio link 

- Links to 1–3 websites you personally built in Kajabi that are most relevant to this project 

A short paragraph (3–5 sentences) commenting on our current site. Note that we will only be transitioning the About Us, Teacher Training Section and Blogs to the new site. 

What works well? 

What is one thing you would improve in the rebuild? 



Applications missing any of the above will not be considered. 

You do not need to submit a full proposal yet — this will be requested only from one or two shortlisted candidates. 



Next Steps 

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to prepare: 

- A fixed-fee proposal for the complete project build 

- An hourly rate for ongoing support and maintenance 





What We Offer 

- Opportunity to shape the online presence of a well-established wellness brand 

- Friendly and collaborative communication (we have two tea
mbers in the Philippines!) 

- Clear brief and efficient project management 

- Potential long-term working relationship after launch 



Additional Considerations 

- Redesign logo or keep current one: Current Logo Link 

- Ensure site is mobile-responsive and optimised for SEO

title: Blog Writer and Pinterest Copywriter for Gift Guide Website
description:
Hi! I'm Mimi, and I run a growing gift guide website that helps people find thoughtful, unique gifts for every occasion. I'm looking for a talented writer to join our small team and help bring this site to life with high-quality gift guide articles and Pinterest copy.



What you'll be doing:

Writing complete gift guide articles (think "Best Gifts for Beach Lovers" or "Unique White Elephant Gifts Under $30") from research through final draft. This includes finding and curating the best products for each guide, writing engaging product descriptions and article copy, writing Pinterest pin titles and descriptions optimized for search, and basic on-page SEO (keywords, meta descriptions, headings).



All content is ghostwritten under the brand's voice and style. You'll be working from article briefs and following our style guide, but I'm looking for someone who can own the full writing process and deliver articles that need only light editing from me before publishing.



You'd be a great fit if you:

Have strong English writing skills with a warm, conversational tone (think talking to a friend, not writing a textbook). Have experience writing product roundups, gift guides, or affiliate content. Understand basic SEO and know how to naturally work keywords into content. Are comfortable doing product research across sites like Amazon, Etsy, and brand websites. Have some familiarity with Pinterest SEO and writing pin descriptions that rank. Are detail-oriented and can follow a style guide consistently. Can work independently and meet deadlines without a lot of hand-holding.



Bonus points if you:

Have experience with WordPress. Have written for lifestyle, gift, or shopping content sites before. Are genuinely interested in gift-giving and love finding cool products.



Benefits and details:

Looking for someone who can work full-time hours. The start date is as soon as possible. You'll get paid with PayPal. The training period is 10 work days. This is your trial period. You'll get 50% pay on day 1 and 50% on day 10. After day 10, you'll be paid once a month. 13th month pay (prorated for your first year). 2 weeks of paid time off per calendar year. 2 weeks of unpaid time off. Time off and paid for all national holidays.



Do you want to work from home with flexible hours? Are you a strong writer who loves finding the perfect gift for people? Do you want a steady role with a small, growing team?



If that sounds like you, apply via this form and tell me what the last great gift you gave someone was (and why you picked it): 



Applications directly via OnlineJobs are ignored.



Thanks so much!

Mimi

California, USA
