title: E-Commerce Inventory & Production Manager (Shopify)
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE





We are a fast-growing, multi-million dollar women’s fashion brand looking for an experienced Inventory & Production Manager to take ownership of our inventory forecasting, restocks, and production coordination.







This role is critical to the business. You will be responsible for ensuring our top-selling products stay in stock, production runs smoothly, and inventory decisions are made based on data — not guesswork.







This role requires someone who can independently manage inventory forecasting and production timelines. This is not a training role.



COMPENSATION





• Starting at $1,800/month (full-time)



• Opportunity for growth based on performance



• Long-term role with a growing global brand



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES





• Manage inventory forecasting using Inventory Planner (or similar tools)



• Monitor sell-through rates and product performance weekly



• Identify restock needs BEFORE products sell out







• Create and manage purchase orders with manufacturers (China & Turkey)



• Confirm ALL production orders before opening any pre-orders



• Track production timelines and follow up with suppliers consistently







• Ensure accurate ETAs are communicated internally



• Prevent overselling or selling inventory that has not been confirmed







• Generate weekly top-seller reports for the marketing and ads team



• Clearly communicate which products should be prioritized for scaling







• Upload and manage products in Shopify including:



– Inventory quantities



– Product naming



– SEO-friendly product descriptions







• Maintain a clean, accurate, and organized Shopify backend



REQUIREMENTS (NON-NEGOTIABLE)





• Proven experience managing inventory for an e-commerce brand



• Strong understanding of inventory forecasting and sell-through analysis



• Experience using Inventory Planner or similar forecasting tools







• Experience working with overseas manufacturers and production timelines



• Strong understanding of Shopify backend







• Ability to work independently and make data-driven decisions



• High attention to detail with minimal errors



• Strong communication and follow-up skills



WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT





• This is not an assistant position



• This is not a beginner role



• This is not a “learn as you go” position







You are expected to take ownership of this function and execute with accuracy.



SUCCESS IN THIS ROLE LOOKS LIKE





• Top-selling products remain in stock



• Restocks are planned proactively (not reactively)



• No pre-orders are opened without confirmed production



• Production timelines are tracked and communicated clearly



• Shopify backend is accurate and well-managed



• Weekly reports directly support sales growth





APPLICATION QUESTIONS (REQUIRED) Please submit answers together with your updated resume at 





Please answer the following:



How do you determine when a product needs to be restocked?

What data or metrics do you use to forecast inventory demand?

Have you used Inventory Planner or a similar tool? Explain how.

How do you prevent stockouts on top-selling products?

Describe your experience working with overseas manufacturers.

What steps do you take before opening a product for pre-order?



FINAL NOTE





We are looking for someone reliable, proactive, and experienced. If you are someone who needs constant direction or training, this role is not the right fit.

title: Virtual Assistant – Bookkeeping & Tax Preparation Support (Mixed Income)
description:
About the Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented Virtual Assistant with bookkeeping experience to help organize 3–5 years of financial records across multiple income sources. The goal is to get everything clean and ready for a CPA to file back tax returns. This is a project-based role with the potential for ongoing work.

Income Sources You’ll Be Working With

 ? W-2 Employment – Contract Scrum Master role (employer-issued wages)

 ? LLC Income – Hairstylist business (self-employed business income, possible business expenses/deductions)

 ? Gig/Delivery Apps – DoorDash, Instacart, Uber Eats, or similar platforms (1099-NEC or platform earnings summaries)

 ? Customer Service Work – Additional contract or freelance income

What You’ll Be Doing

 ? Gathering and organizing income records across all four income types for 3–5 tax years

 ? Categorizing business expenses for the LLC (supplies, chair rental, products, mileage, etc.)

 ? Tracking mileage and delivery-related expenses for gig platform work

 ? Reconciling income from W-2s, 1099s, and gig platform payout reports

 ? Building a clean year-by-year income and expense summary ready for a CPA

 ? Flagging any missing documents, gaps, or inconsistencies and communicating them clearly

What We’re Looking For

 ? Experience bookkeeping for individuals with mixed or self-employed income (W-2 + 1099 + LLC)

 ? Familiarity with US tax concepts: W-2s, 1099-NECs, Schedule C, self-employment tax

 ? Experience with gig platform income (DoorDash, Uber, Instacart, etc.) is a strong plus

 ? Proficient in QuickBooks, Wave, FreshBooks, or Excel/Google Sheets

 ? Strong attention to detail — able to spot duplicate entries, missing months, or mismatched totals

 ? Excellent written English communication

 ? Reliable and trustworthy with sensitive financial data

 ? Experience supporting US-based clients preferred

Project Scope

 ? Duration: Project-based (estimated 4-8 weeks depending on volume of records)

 ? Hours: Flexible, approximately 10–20 hours/week

 ? Time Zone: Flexible, but must be available for check-ins during US business hours

How to Apply

Please send the following:

 1. A brief introduction and your bookkeeping background

 2. Specific experience with mixed-income clients (W-2 + self-employed + gig work)

 3. Your preferred tools/software

 4. Your availability and hourly rate

title: Experienced Credit Repair Disputer (FCRA & FDCPA Expert)
description:
Experienced Credit Repair Disputer (FCRA & FDCPA Expert)



**Job Description:**



We are looking for a **skilled, proactive, and highly organized Credit Repair Disputer** to join our growing credit repair team. The ideal candidate must have **strong experience with U.S. credit laws, dispute strategies, and credit bureau processes**.



This role requires someone who is detail-oriented, knowledgeable, and capable of handling disputes professionally while maintaining accurate documentation for every client file.



**Responsibilities:**



* Review client credit reports from all three bureaus (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion)

* Identify **inaccuracies, inconsistencies, and violations**

* Write **personalized dispute letters** for each client and each dispute round

* Include **screenshots of the credit report highlighting the specific inaccuracies** being challenged

* Track disputes and responses from credit bureaus and creditors

* **Rewrite and refresh dispute letters every 90 days** to ensure they pass through the **ACDV system used by credit bureaus**

* Maintain organized client files and dispute history

* Communicate professionally with the team regarding dispute progress

* Follow **FCRA and FDCPA compliance standards**



**Requirements:**



* Strong knowledge of **FCRA and FDCPA laws**

* Proven experience in **credit repair dispute processing**

* Ability to write **custom dispute letters (not templates)**

* Understanding of how the **ACDV system works**

* Excellent **organization and attention to detail**

* Ability to **highlight and document inaccuracies with screenshots**

* Strong written English skills

* Professional, reliable, and easy to work with



**Preferred Experience:**



* Experience working with **credit repair companies**

* Familiarity with **Metro 2 reporting**

* Knowledge of common dispute strategies and compliance practices



**How to Apply:**



Please include:



1. Your **experience with credit repair disputes**

2. Which **laws and dispute strategies you typically use**

3. A **sample dispute letter you have written**

4. The credit repair software you have used (if any)



We are looking for someone **long-term who takes pride in doing detailed, high-quality dispute work.**

title: AI Voice Developer - Production-Ready Voice Agents & SMS Automation
description:
We're ToneHaus, an AI voice agency building custom voice AI systems for trades and SMBs. We're looking for a production-ready AI voice developer to build out 2 voice AI systems and 1 SMS automation agent starting Monday.



This is not entry-level. We need someone with proven experience shipping production voice agents, not proof of concepts or tutorials.



THE PROJECT

Phase 1 (Weeks 2-3):



- Build 2 custom voice AI agents (inbound call handling, lead qualification, appointment booking)

- Build 1 SMS automation system (lead follow-up, confirmations, notifications)

- Integration with existing CRM/booking platforms (Payaca, BigChange and ServiceM8)

- Testing and refinement



Phase 2 (Ongoing):

If Phase 1 goes well, we have a consistent pipeline of client projects through our agency. This could be 20-40 hours/week of ongoing work with long-term stability.



WE NEED

? Proven experience building production voice AI agents (not tutorials, not demos)

? Evidence of work: active phone number from a past client, backend system walkthrough, case study, or similar

? Experience with voice/telephony platforms (11Labs, Retell AI, Twilio, etc.)

? Understanding of LLMs and prompt engineering

? Experience with n8n or similar automation platforms

? Ability to integrate with CRM/booking systems (Calendly, HubSpot, Airtable, etc.)

? Reliable communication and organised workflow



WHAT WE'LL ASK IN YOUR APPLICATION

Before applying, be prepared to answer:



Your Voice AI Experience: Walk us through a production system you built. What was the business problem? What tools did you use? What were the results?



Your Evidence: Provide one of the following:



An active phone number from a past client's business (we'll call it to test)

A backend system walkthrough (Loom video or call) showing your architecture

A detailed case study with metrics

Links to deployed systems you've built



Your Building Process: 



- How do you approach building a new voice AI system? Walk us through your workflow from requirements to deployment.



- Testing & Quality Assurance: How do you test voice agents? What does your QA process look like? How do you catch edge cases?



- Your Tech Stack: What LLMs do you use? (Claude, GPT-4, etc.) What voice platforms? What do you use for automation and integration? Why those choices?



- Communication & Organisation: How do you communicate progress? What tools do you use to stay organised? How do you handle revisions and feedback?



- Your Experience: Years building voice AI? What types of businesses have you worked with? What's the most complex system you've built?





WHY THIS MATTERS

We work with real businesses: plumbers, HVAC contractors, med spas, roofing companies. Their customers are calling expecting a real experience. We need someone who:



- Ships production-quality code

- Tests thoroughly

- Thinks about edge cases and user experience

- Communicates clearly and promptly

- Can scale with us



WHAT'S NEXT?



If you have solid production experience and real evidence of work, apply with:



- A brief intro

- Answers to the 7 questions above

- Your evidence (link, phone number, or Loom video)



We'll review applications and start conversations with people who meet the requirements.

title: AI VIDEO EXPERT [Need to be great at prompting]
description:
AI Video Creator — Realistic UGC-Style Video for App Ads

We run a portfolio of consumer apps and need someone who can generate hyper-realistic, casual UGC-style video using Sora, VEO, or Kling. The output needs to look like a real person filmed it on their phone — not like AI. If it looks AI-generated, it's useless to us.

Think: someone casually talking about an app, using a product in their kitchen, walking through a store, reacting to something on their phone. That level of realism is what we need.



The Work



Generate realistic AI video for app ads and organic TikTok based on briefs and scripts we provide

Stay obsessively up to date on the latest prompting techniques, model updates, and workflows for Sora, VEO, and Kling — these tools evolve weekly and we need someone who's on top of every update

Know which tool to use for which type of output and why

Iterate fast until the output passes as real casual footage





Must-Haves



Proven experience generating realistic human video with Sora, VEO, or Kling (not Runway, not Pika — we're specifically looking for these three)

You actively follow model updates, new techniques, and community discoveries for these tools — if we ask you what changed in the last update, you should know

Output that consistently passes as real phone-shot UGC, not polished AI demos

Weekend availability (Saturday + Sunday required)

Quick turnarounds


 communication via
 — must understand English

Reliable internet



Nice-to-Haves



Basic editing skills to trim and polish AI output

Experience creating content specifically for mobile app ads

Active in AI video communities 
/X, 
, Reddit) where new methods get shared first





Pay

Competitive monthly rate paid via Paypal. Long-term ongoing role.



Work hours:

You can work during phillipine time.



How to Apply

Applications that skip these steps get ignored.

Subject line must include the word RED.

Answer these questions in your message:



Which of the three tools (Sora, VEO, Kling) are you strongest with and why?

What's one specific prompting technique or workflow you use to make AI video look less like AI?

How do you stay current on updates and new methods for these tools?

Confirm you can work Saturdays and Sundays.



Send 3+ examples of realistic, casual-style AI video you generated. Direct links or files only. If we can immediately tell it's AI, you won't move forward.



What Happens Next

Qualified applicants get a paid test — a brief and a deadline. You get paid regardless of outcome.

Hiring asap.

title: FULLTIME Question Curator for Instagram (Remote| PH-based VA)
description:
We’re looking for a full-time Question Curator to join our team and help create high-performing question content for social media.



You’ll be working on content for our IG accounts under Flamingo Cards. This role is ideal for someone with strong judgment, great writing instincts, and an eye for what makes people want to answer (and share).



You do NOT need to be a “professional writer.” We care more about taste, structure, safety, and consistency. We’ll train you on our formats and what performs best.



What you’ll be doing:

*Curating and writing question sets for IG carousel posts

*Creating questions for themes like couples, friends, fun, deep, wholesome, etc.

*Turning a prompt/topic into 5 questions with intentional progression

*Making revisions based on feedback and performance data (link taps)



What we’re looking for:

*Strong English writing skills (simple, natural, not “AI sounding”)

*Excellent question judgment (knows what’s intriguing but still safe)

*Ability to follow formats and keep a consistent brand voice

*Taste for internet-friendly, scroll-stopping prompts (without being cringe)

*Comfortable writing lots of variations without repeating yourself

*Detail-oriented (spot duplicates, awkward phrasing, or overly similar prompts)

*Reliable internet connection

title: Virtual Assistant / Operations Coordinator (Support Two Executives)| Part-Time (5–10 hrs/wk to start, with growth
description:
About the Role



We are seeking a highly capable, detail-oriented Virtual Assistant / Operations Coordinator to support two executives across a portfolio of business ventures and personal and family responsibilities.



This is not a typical admin role.



You will act as a central operator—bringing structure, clarity, and execution across multiple moving parts. You will work closely with both principals to ensure priorities are managed, communication flows seamlessly, and nothing falls through the cracks.



If you are proactive, highly organised, and take ownership of outcomes—not just tasks—this role will suit you.



What Success Looks Like

• Calendars, travel, and family commitments run seamlessly

• Financial admin and reporting are accurate and up to date

• Communication across projects is clear, timely, and proactive

• We spend less time on coordination, and more time on high-value work

• You anticipate needs before being asked



Key Responsibilities

Executive & Administrative Support

• Manage complex calendars across two executives, including prioritisation

• Draft, edit, and manage communications 
s, memos, presentations)

• Coordinate meetings, agendas, and follow-ups

• Act as a key point of contact across stakeholders



Operations & Project Support

• Support multiple business initiatives (joint and individual)

• Track tasks, deadlines, and deliverables across projects

• Maintain organised systems for documents and workflows

• Improve processes and suggest efficiencies where possible



Financial Administration (No direct handling of funds)

• Track expenses, invoices, and recurring payments

• Assist with account reconciliation and reporting (Xero or similar)

• Maintain accurate financial records and dashboards

• Prepare information for review and approval



Personal & Lifestyle Support

• Travel planning (business and personal)

• Appointment scheduling

• General life admin to support a high-performing household



About You

You are not just organised—you are structured in how you think.



You bring:

• Proven experience as a Virtual Assistant or Executive Assistant (minimum 2–3 years)

• Strong written and verbal English communication

• High attention to detail and reliability

• Ability to manage multiple priorities across different stakeholders

• A proactive mindset—you solve problems before being asked

• Comfort working with ambiguity and bringing clarity

• High level of discretion and professionalism



Tools & Systems Experience

You are comfortable with (or quick to learn):

• Google Workspace / Microsoft Office

• Xero or similar accounting tools

• Project management tools (e.g. Notion, Asana, ClickUp)

• AI tools (e.g. ChatGPT) to improve efficiency



Working Arrangement

• Remote (must be within ±3 hours of AEST / GMT+10)

• Starting at 5–10 hours per week, with potential to scale

• Supporting two executives across shared and individual priorities



Compensation

• $12–18 USD/hour, depending on experience

• Opportunity for increased hours and rate based on performance

• Long-term role for the right candidate



Application Process

To apply, please follow instructions carefully:

1. Start your application with:

“I am the Awesome VA you need”

2. Include only the following:

a. Describe your previous Virtual Assistant / Executive Assistant experience

b. Provide two professional references (company +
—we will verify)

c. What score (1–10) would your previous employer give you, and why?

d. What tools and systems have you used regularly?

e. Describe a time you improved a process or system for your employer

f. Your availability (hours per week and preferred working times)

g. Your earliest start date



Interview Process

1. Application review

2. Video interview (we will assess communication, attention to detail, and work setup)

3. Paid trial period (1 week)



Final Note

We are looking for someone who wants to grow with us long-term.



If you take pride in your work, think ahead, and enjoy bringing order to complexity—we’d like to hear from you.

title: Video Editor - $1,500-2,000+/Month Performance Based Fast-Growing Supplement Brand
description:
We're hiring video editors for Blissta, a supplement brand  is scaling fast. We need editors who can take a script and turn it into a winning ad. Not tomorrow. Today.



**What You'll Do**



Take ad briefs from our creative team and bring them to life. Every script comes with timestamps, visual direction, voiceover, and pacing notes. Your job is to execute it exactly. Every detail matters. The smallest thing can be the difference between an ad that spends $50K and one that gets turned off.



Edit 2+ video ads per day. Source b-roll from YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, stock sites. Find clips that match the script, not random footage. Use AI tools like HeyGen, ElevenLabs, Higgsfield, Flow AI to generate visuals, voiceovers, and animations.



Work directly with our Creative Director. They brief you. You produce. They review. You revise. Nothing goes live until it's perfect.



**Pay Structure**



Starting base: $1,500-2,000/month depending on experience. Performance bonuses kick in week 2. Top editors earn 2,000+ within 60 days based on output and ad performance. If your videos start spending in our ad account your pay goes up immediately. No ceiling. We promote fast. We pay fast.



**What You Need To Know**



How to source footage strategically from YouTube, Pexels, Storyblocks, TikTok, Instagram. How to match visuals to voiceover. Not random b-roll. Intentional clips that match the exact 
nt in the script. Pacing for direct response ads. Fast, punchy, no dead space. Text and caption placement that's readable and scroll-stopping. Basic color correction and audio cleanup. AI tools for video generation, avatar cloning, voiceover. Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, or CapCut.



**What We Provide**



All scripts written for you with full timestamps and visual direction. AI tools for video generation and editing. Stock footage subscriptions. Fast feedback loops so you get better quickly. Long-term work if you're good. A creative team that actually knows what converts.



**Qualifications**



1+ year editing video ads. Not vlogs. Not YouTube videos. Ads. Experience with CapCut, Premiere Pro, or DaVinci Resolve. Experience editing eCommerce or direct response ads. Experience with AI video tools is a huge plus.



**This Is NOT For You If**



You think creative means slow transitions and mood lighting. You can't take feedback. You miss deadlines. You think one video per day is enough. You need hand-holding. You're not hungry.



**This IS For You If**



You're fast and you're hungry. You want to get paid based on what you produce not how many hours you sit at your desk. You understand direct response. You want to work with a team that's scaling something real and you want to grow with it.



**Growth Potential**



Get priority on editing the highest budget ads. Higher pay as you prove yourself. Creative Director path to lead and manage other editors.



**How To Apply**



Send us:

1. Link to 2-3 direct response video ads you've edited. Not brand videos. Not vlogs. Ads.

2. A 2 sentence explanation of why each ad works.

3. Your availability and monthly rate.



Subject line: "Blissta Video Editor — I edit ads that convert"

title: Full-Stack Developer - Upgrade Admin Panel, Client Panel and Mobile App
description:
We are MediTech, a healthcare-focused marketplace platform connecting patients with clinics and medical service providers. We have an existing source code base and are looking for an experienced full-stack developer (or small agency) to upgrade both our Admin Panel, Seller (Clinic) Panel and mobile app - removing irrelevant features, renaming sections to match our medical/booking context, and adding powerful new features including third-party booking API integrations, a no-show fee system, referral codes, payment state management, and more.



The ideal candidate must have proven experience integrating third-party calendar and booking platform APIs (such as Fresha, Booksy, Vagaro, and Aoikumo) with existing booking systems, ensuring seamless two-way sync between external platforms and our own.



The candidate should be able to connect to each platform's live availability feed — subscribing to real-time schedule data so that open slots are automatically surfaced on our platform as they become available. When a customer books through MediTech, that booking must be pushed back to the clinic's original platform to prevent double bookings and keep all systems in sync.



The candidate must also be able to architect and implement a dynamic discount rules engine, where clinic owners can configure time-based pricing tiers (for example, 24 hours before a slot = 15% off, 5 hours before = 20% off). This includes building a scheduled job system that automatically monitors upcoming appointment slots, applies the correct discount tier at the right time, and handles edge cases such as overlapping rules, discount stacking, and override logic — ensuring pricing is always accurate and applied without manual intervention.



The Current Tech stack: 

* Laravel powers our backend.

* Flutter runs our mobile apps.

* Reactjs empowers the Admin Panels.

* Typescript and Nextjs drives our high-performance website.

* To ensure smooth interactions and seamless integration, our platform is backed by Rest API.



I’m looking for someone to execute my requirements within a one-month timeline, including all revisions and changes needed along the way.



Upon successful completion of the project, I’d be open to offering a contract based role within my company, where you would be responsible for ongoing maintenance and support.



Send me your resume + portfolio. Looking forward to find the right match to work with me.

description:
Meta Ads Creative Designer – Acoustia (Part-time, with fast-track to Full-time)

Acoustia is a fast-growing Dutch e-commerce brand selling affordable, discrete in-ear hearing aids to customers in the Netherlands and Belgium. We sell directly online and advertise aggressively on
 and Instagram to reach our target audience — mostly seniors and their families who are looking for a high-quality hearing solution at a fair price.

We are looking for a talented Creative Designer to join our team and produce high-performing Meta ad creatives. You will start part-time, and if the quality of your work is strong, we will move to full-time quickly.

What you'll be making:

You will be producing around 10–15 creatives per day to start, increasing over time. This includes static image ads, video ads, reels, carousels, and stories — all in Dutch, as we advertise in the Netherlands and Belgium. If you specialize specifically in statics or video, you are still very welcome to apply.

How we work:

We move fast. We will provide you with the strategy behind each creative, and ad copy when needed. We will also share references from competitors so you always have clear direction. In return, we expect you to meet deadlines without being reminded — we advertise at high speed and timing matters.

Any tools are fine — we use Canva Pro, Claude and Higgsfield ourselves, but what matters is the quality of the output

title: Editing VA -Part-time Capcut / TikTok Editor with potential for more
description:
Part-time Capcut / TikTok Editor with potential for more



Hiring for part-time work for a Capcut / TikTok editor



You will be making TikTok videos using Capcut.  The editing process is fairly simple and straightforward.  



Anyone who is proficient in video editing should have no problem with the basic tasks such as:



Cutting dead space in talking head videos

Adding overlay images on screen in the videos

Minor effects such as dynamic zoom, cropping, etc.

Producing, sorting and compiling b-roll content from voiceover and footage provided, having the ability to digest what is being said and pair up the audio with corresponding footage. These are typically fast cuts, that need to be highly engaging. Lots of movement but simple edits. These will be for Tiktok videos and eventually we will repurpose to other platforms.



Please review a few examples of my content to ensure you are familiar and comfortable with the editing style:



Basic talking head - here you are mostly editing dead space and piecing clips together, adding minor effects like crop and zoom to make the frames look more dynamic and seamless.













Talking head with overlays - same as before, but adding images provided to you on the screen to support what is being said in the video.







B-roll content - you will be provided a voice over and need to work through the footage to find fitting visuals. A strong understanding of English and social media content is crucial here.











Skit work- typically these are one take but a lot of times we need to cut videos together if there are mistakes. You need to be able to recognize those mistakes and clear them out to make it a seamless video. 

















You will be working hand in hand with me on an extremely viral social media page and there is room for bonuses if deadlines are met and the content performs well!



Requirements:

Attention to detail

STRONG English

Strong internet connection

Previous experience with Capcut / TikTok / Instagram

Good working with a team

Ability to learn and take constructive feedback

Availability to work 3-4h a day (with potential for more and increased salary)

Able to work 5-6 days per week (can take days off whenever as long as you are consistent and videos are being completed)

Can take payments through Wise

PH or similar time zone



$300-$400/month + bonuses for 3-4h a day MAX and potential full time work in the future with a salary increase.



To apply, please complete the recreate assignment below so I can see your skills (1h max)



Below is a Google drive with the original files you will need to re-create with a reference video: Please choose 2 



Google drive:

Broll: 

Talking Head: 

Product Video: 





Download the files and import into your video editing software

Please complete the following steps:



Broll: 

Download the files and import into your video editing software

Find the “good” takes of the audio sections and stitch together to complete the voiceover

Find the corresponding footage to match what is being said in the voiceover

Pay close attention to detail in the selection of the clips, the pacing (duration of each scene), cropping, etc.

Message me with a link to the re-created video 



Talking Head: 

Download the files and import into your video editing software

Cut out all dead space between videos

Each clip should flow smoothly into the next with minimal to no pause between words

Tip: cut the clip .2-.3 seconds shorter than the end of the word said.

Do not use transitions between clips, static clips only.

Message me a link to re-create video



Product Video: 

Download the files and import into your video editing software

Cut out all dead space between videos

Each clip should flow smoothly into the next with minimal to no pause between words

Tip: cut the clip .2-.3 seconds shorter than the end of the word said.

Using the reference video and context, place overlays in the proper spots

Pay attention to overlay position and length

Make them big enough to see/read, but not so big they dominate the screen

Do not use any transitions, static overlays only

Message me a link to re-create video





Please Respond back to me beginning with the word “blue”

title: Performance Video Editor for Winning Meta Ads
description:
We’re looking for a highly skilled Performance Video Editor to help us iterate on winning Meta video ads.



This is not a role focused on creating brand films or editing generic social content. Your main job is to take proven winning ad concepts and turn them into high-quality variations that improve engagement, retention, clarity, and freshness, without damaging what made the ad work in the first place.



You will work with existing winning creatives that we hand to you. We will usually provide the strategic direction, testing hypothesis, new hooks when relevant, and structured feedback. We are looking for someone who can execute at a high level, collaborate well, and bring strong performance-editing instincts to the table.



What you’ll do

- Create variations of existing winning Meta video ads

- Improve pacing, tighten edits, and shorten videos where needed

- Add VFX, sound effects, motion emphasis, and visual polish to key moments

- Add and refine subtitles, captions, text overlays, and on-screen hierarchy

- Integrate B-roll and additional voice-over when provided

- Clean up audio and improve overall sound quality

- Clean up cuts and make edits feel sharper and more intentional

- Deliver polished, finished 9:16 exports optimized for Meta paid social

- Revise quickly and accurately based on structured feedback

- Occasionally suggest smart edit improvements beyond the brief, with reasoning



What this role is focused on

This role is about iterating on winners, not reinventing them.



We want someone who understands how to:

- preserve the winning core of an ad

- create controlled variations tied to a specific testing hypothesis

- improve engagement in different ways through editing

- make key moments land harder without overediting

- balance direct-response performance with high production quality



What we’ll provide

- The winning concept to iterate on

- A clear brief and testing hypothesis for each variation

- New hooks when needed

- Ideas for moments to emphasize

- Additional VO, B-roll direction, or text direction when relevant

- Structured feedback for revisions

- Our Brand Book with brand direction



What we need from you

- Strong experience editing performance creative / direct-response ads

- Strong understanding of Meta paid social

- Excellent pacing and retention instincts

- Advanced editing skills beyond basic raw UGC cutting

- Strong subtitle and text-overlay design sense

- Strong VFX / SFX / motion enhancement instincts

- Clean audio editing and finishing ability

- Ability to follow a brief while also contributing thoughtful ideas

- Strong organization, file hygiene, and version control

- Ability to deliver finished work at a consistently high standard



Important constraints

- The first 3 seconds are generally off-limits in terms of changing the underlying footage

- Effects, polish, and editing improvements can be added, but the core opening footage usually stays

- These are hypothesis-driven variations, not random creative remixes

- Revisions are part of the process and should not be an issue

- Deliverables must be high quality, with Meta-optimized resolutions and exports



Ideal profile

You’re likely a strong fit if you:

- have edited for DTC brands or performance agencies

- understand retention, payoff, proof, pacing, and direct-response structure

- know how to make ads feel fresher without changing the core concept too much

- can collaborate with founders / marketers and improve execution quality

- understand that every edit should serve performance, not just aesthetics



Nice to have

- Experience with supplement, wellness, health, or consumer product brands

- Ability to work in Premiere Pro and After Effects

- Ability to source or create tasteful supporting motion / visual enhancement where needed

- Familiarity with version testing workflows



Working setup

- Freelance to start

- Potential for regular ongoing work / retainer if the fit is strong

- Work will be assigned on a concept-by-concept basis around winning ads we want to scale or refresh



To apply

Please send:

- a short introduction

- relevant examples of performance / direct-response ad edits

- examples where you created variations of an existing winning concept

- the software you use

- your typical turnaround times

- your rate structure

- whether you are open to a paid trial task



When applying, include the phrase: “controlled variation beats random editing” so we know you read the full brief.

title: WEB DEVELOPER - WordPress/Elementor
description:
WordPress / Elementor Developer — Freelance 



We’re looking for an experienced WordPress/Elementor developer to build two websites from provided design files. This is a freelance, remote role billed hourly. Candidates based anywhere are welcome, but you must be available for at least a few hours overlap with UK business hours (GMT/BST) for communication and collaboration. 

 

About the project 

The Shala yoga studio and The Shala School of Yoga were once under the same directorship and shared a single website (
). They are now two separate businesses, each with their own director. The shared site needs to be separated into two distinct sites: 

— 
 — for Vahni at The Shala  for  yoga studio classes and workshops 

— The Shala School of Yoga under theshalalondon address, for teacher trainings  

 

The project will be divided into two phases as follows and you will bill the two businesses respectively  

 

PHASE 1 – VAHNI  

What you’ll be doing 

— Archiving the existing site properly so all data is retained and retrievable  

 

— Building a WordPress/Elementor Pro site from provided design files  (Figma/HTML mockups)

 

— Working closely with our team on a clear design handoff and building faithfully to that spec 

 

— Migrating and organising content from the existing shared WordPress site into the new site 

 

— Integrating Momence as the booking platform in the same way it is integrated currently 

 

— Setting up a dynamic events/workshop system with SEO-optimised content in WordPress (not reliant on Momence for indexing) 

 

— Implementing SEO migration — preserving existing URLs, 301 redirects, and applying meta data provided by our SEO team 

 

— Ensuring strong UX throughout — the sites must be clean, fast, accessible, and easy to navigate 

 

PHASE 2 – THE SHALA SCHOOL BUILD 

What you’ll be doing 

— Building a WordPress/Elementor Pro site from provided HTML templates 

 

— Working closely with the team on any updates/design tweaks 

 

— Migrating and organising content from the existing WordPress site into the new site 

 

— Integrating Momence as the booking platform – the integration here is very straightforward. 

 

— Implementing SEO migration — preserving existing URLs, 301 redirects, and applying meta data provided by our SEO team 

 

— Ensuring strong UX throughout — the site must be clean, fast, accessible, and easy to navigate  

 

 

What we’re looking for:

title: Telecom AutoCAD Permit Drafter (Remote – Telecom Experience Required)
description:
About TESINC, LLC

TESINC, LLC is a fast-growing broadband engineering company specializing in telecom and utility design. We support U.S.-based telecom infrastructure projects and are expanding our remote drafting team.



We are looking for a reliable, experienced Telecom AutoCAD Drafter who wants consistent, long-term work and the opportunity to grow with a strong and supportive team.



Job Overview

You will create permit-ready AutoCAD drawings for telecom infrastructure projects, including fiber networks, utility poles, underground conduits, and small cell deployments.



You’ll collaborate closely with engineers and project managers to produce accurate, high-quality drawings that meet U.S. permitting standards.



Responsibilities

- Create permit-ready AutoCAD drawings for telecom projects

- Revise drawings based on redlines and feedback

- Ensure compliance with telecom standards and local regulations

- Maintain accuracy, consistency, and attention to detail across all drawings

- Communicate with the team to meet deadlines and project requirements



Required Qualifications

- Strong experience with AutoCAD drafting (telecom or utility focus)

- Experience with fiber, OSP, utility poles, and underground conduit design

- Proven experience creating permit-ready drawings

- Understanding of U.S. telecom permitting processes (or similar)

- Strong skills in AutoCAD (layers, plotting, drafting standards)

- Excellent attention to detail and ability to follow standards

- Strong English communication skills

- Ability to work independently in a remote setting



Nice to Have

- Experience working on U.S. telecom projects

- Background in Civil or Electrical Engineering

- Familiarity with GIS tools (ArcGIS, QGIS)

- Experience with Civil 3D or similar tools



What We Offer

- Long-term, stable work opportunity

- Competitive hourly compensation

- Flexible remote work setup

- Training and onboarding support

- Opportunity to grow with a fast-scaling company

- Collaborative and professional team environment



How to Apply

Please submit:

- Your resume

- Relevant AutoCAD work samples (telecom drawings or permit sets)



In your message, include:

- A brief description of a telecom project you’ve worked on

- Your experience with permit drawings



Send to: 



We’re looking for someone who values quality, consistency, and long-term collaboration. If you’re experienced in telecom drafting and want a reliable, ongoing opportunity with a growing team, we’d love to work with you.

title: Amazon Manager
description:
Amazon & Ecommerce Manager — MySweetSmile (Remote, Full-Time)



ABOUT US



MySweetSmile is an award-winning teeth whitening and oral care brand with over 1,000,000 customers globally. We're the UK's #1 teeth whitening brand (The Independent, 2023 & 2024), sold across Amazon UK, US, and EU, Shopify, TikTok Shop, and multiple retail marketplaces. We're growing fast and need someone to own our marketplace operations full-time.



THE ROLE



You'll be the person who lives inside our Amazon accounts and marketplace platforms every single day. Your job is to ensure every listing, every market, and every backend detail is optimised, accurate, and up to date — so the founder doesn't have to.

This is not a VA role. This is full ownership of marketplace operations across multiple platforms and countries.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN



Amazon (Primary — 50% of role)

- Listing management across UK, US, DE, FR, ES, IT, NL, SE, PL — titles, bullet points, descriptions, backend keywords, search terms, product categories

- A+ Content creation, Brand Story updates, and image gallery management

- Flat file uploads and feed file management

- Listing experiments (A/B testing titles, images, bullets)

- Promotions setup and management (coupons, deals, Brand Tailored Promotions)

- Backend compliance: ingredients, directions for use, safety warnings, category accuracy

- Translations and localisation for EU marketplaces (using AI tools is expected)

- Keeping all marketplaces aligned to a universal standard set by the founder



New Product Launches & Marketplace Expansion (15% of role)

- End-to-end listing creation for new MySweetSmile products across all active marketplaces — content, A+ pages, compliance, and go-live readiness

- Facilitating new marketplace launches as we expand into UAE, Saudi Arabia, Japan, Singapore, and beyond — account setup, listing creation, marketplace-specific requirements, and ensuring all content meets local standards

- Working with the founder and operations team to coordinate launch timelines



Other Marketplaces (10% of role)

- TikTok Shop listing & store management

- Superdrug, Debenhams, OnBuy, and other retail marketplace listings

- Ensuring product data, imagery, and pricing is consistent across all channels



Inventory & Stock Coordination (10% of role)

- Monitoring FBA stock levels daily across all marketplaces

- Flagging restock requirements to the operations team before stockouts occur

- Coordinating on inbound shipment timelines and inventory planning

- Tracking stock positions across non-Amazon channels



Reporting & Competitor Monitoring (10% of role)

- Weekly marketplace performance report summarising sales, stock levels, and key metrics across all channels

- Reviewing advertising agency performance and flagging concerns to the founder

- Weekly competitor monitoring — tracking pricing, listing changes, and new product activity in our category



Creative & Design (5% of role)

- A+ Content design and image gallery creation in Canva

- Updating lifestyle and product imagery across listings

- Graphic design for marketplace-specific assets



WHAT YOU MUST HAVE — NON-NEGOTIABLE



If you don't have all of the following, this role is not for you.



- 3+ years of hands-on Amazon Seller Central experience — you know flat files, backend fields, feed management, and catalogue operations inside out. Not just UI editing.

- Experience managing listings across at least 5 Amazon marketplaces

- Experience launching products on new Amazon marketplaces (especially UAE, JP, SA, SG, AU)

- Confident with A+ Content, Brand Registry, and Brand Story creation

- Confident with Canva or similar design tools for A+ content, image galleries, and basic graphic design

- Experience with TikTok Shop or other non-Amazon marketplace platforms

- Experience with Shopify (product page updates, imagery, descriptions, pricing)

- Experience with Helium 10, Jungle Scout, or similar Amazon tools

- Basic understanding of Amazon PPC — enough to review an agency's performance, flag issues, and ask the right questions

- Experience with European marketplace translations and localisation

- Experience using AI tools (Claude, etc.) for copywriting, translations, and productivity — this is how we work and you'll be expected to use them daily

- Amazon compliance knowledge: ingredients, origin documentation, safety warnings, category requirements

- Strong written English

- Self-starter who takes full ownership and doesn't wait to be told what to do

- Detail-obsessed — you spot a wrong bullet point, a missing keyword, or an incorrect category and it bothers you



TOOLS WE USE

-Amazon Seller Central (global), Canva, Shopify, Notion, Slack, Google Sheets, Google Drive, Helium 10, AI tools (Claude)



SCHEDULE



Flexible within UK business hours overlap. We need at least 4–5 hours of crossover with UK time (GMT/BST) daily. Full-time, 50 hours per week, 6 days (1 fixed day off).



COMPENSATION



Depending on experience. Long-term role with room to grow as the brand scales into new markets and product lines.



THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR YOU IF:



- You only know basic VA-level Amazon tasks

- You've never worked with flat files or feed management

- You don't have marketplace expansion experience

- You can't design A+ content or work in Canva

- You need hand-holding or frequent check-ins

- You've never used AI tools for productivity

- You can't work independently across time zones

- You want a regular 9-5. 



HOW TO APPLY



Title your application: "MSS Amazon & Ecommerce Manager — [Your Name]"



Include:

- A short intro (3–4 sentences) on why you're the right fit

- A link to a Google Sheet or Doc showing your Amazon marketplace experience — specifically which marketplaces you've managed, what tasks you handled, and any results you achieved

- One example of A+ Content or an Amazon listing you've optimised (screenshot or link)



Applications that don't follow these specific steps will not be reviewed.

title: Video Editor who #Believes in Entrepreneurs| Flexible Work Schedule
description:
We're trying to solve the world's biggest problem: People don't #Believe in themselves enough. For over 22 years, Evan Carmichael has been dedicated to this mission. The way we're approaching this problem is producing inspirational content at scale. The right video at the right time can completely change someone's life and we want to touch as many lives as possible. People may not have a supportive family or community around them, but our content can help give them the belief they need to chase their dreams and make the world a better place. We want to work with people who also want to make an impact and touch people's lives. In your application, please put ‘I am your next awesome Video Editor!’ in the subject line. Please apply through OnlineJobsPh and not via
. The job responsibilities include editing the first minute of videos to grab and keep the audience's attention, selecting the most engaging clips, adding eye-catching transitions and effects, ensuring the sound and visuals are top-notch, and staying on top of the latest trends to keep things fresh. Our goal is to get to 70% audience retention in the first minute. Additionally, strong creativity, attention to detail, and effective communication skills are essential to produce high-quality videos that engage viewers and meet retention goals. Please also share the link to your resume and portfolio, what your hourly salary expectations are, and in the first line of your message tell us how many YouTube subscribers Evan Carmichael has, followed by who your favorite entrepreneur of all time is and why. The job is full time (40 hours per week) with a flexible schedule. We would start off with a small project for now and we'd pay you for your ideas and research. We also like to work with people over a long period of time, not just short contracts, and give them opportunities to succeed and do more based on what they want to learn. Hopefully that will be you! Thank you for reading and good luck! Sending lots of love! -Irish #Believe

title: VIDEO EDITOR
description:
Before you apply, read this carefully.

We run one of the fastest-growing pet brands online.

DTC. Performance-focused. High volume.

And we're looking for a Video Editor who actually knows what they're doing.

Not someone who cuts clips together.

Someone who makes people stop scrolling.

What the job is:

You receive a script. You deliver a scroll-stopping, emotion-driven video ad. Within 24 hours.

That's it. 

What we need from you:

? Strong editing skills (pacing, sound design, motion, storytelling)

? AI tools fluency : you must be actively using AI in your workflow (voiceover, generation, enhancement,.. we don't care which, but you need to know them)

? Consistency. Speed. No excuses.

? Available for weekly projects, long-term

You are NOT a fit if:

? You apply with a generic message

? You've never edited a performance ad before

? You need 3 days to deliver a 60-second video

? You don't use AI tools in your process



The Hiring Process:



Trial Task : 45 to 60 sec video from our script. 24h delivery. Unpaid. This filters out everyone who's not serious.

Paid Project Trial : Full brand video. If it's good, short interview follows.

One-Month Paid Trial : Weekly projects with feedback.

Long-Term Offer : For those who consistently deliver.





ONE MORE THING.

We get a lot of applications.

Most of them are copy-paste. Vague. Useless.

If yours looks like that, we won't even open it.

To apply, your message must follow this exact structure :



Full Name:

Age:

City:

Years of experience in video editing:

AI tools you actively use:

Link to past video ads (not general reels, ad content):

One sentence: why should we pick you over everyone else?

Loom Video (2-3 min, in English, introduce yourself):

If your message doesn't follow this structure exactly, it gets ignored.

We're not harsh. We're just focused.

The right person will love this.

Job Title

We run a Meta ads service producing short-form video ads for service-based businesses — window cleaning, house cleaning, and similar home service niches. We're hiring immediately and looking to get started right away.

title: Personal Trainer (virtual)
description:
Personal Trainer & Health Coach (Part-Time, Remote | Functional Medicine Focus)

We are a growing health & wellness company specializing in functional medicine, thyroid optimization, and metabolic health. We are looking for a certified Personal Trainer / Health Coach who can support clients holistically—combining fitness, nutrition, and lifestyle coaching for individuals with chronic conditions such as thyroid dysfunction and diabetes.

This is a hybrid role combining coaching, communication, and light content support.

Role Overview

You will work closely with clients inside a structured program, helping them:

Improve energy, metabolism, and body composition

Build sustainable fitness and lifestyle habits

Navigate chronic conditions (thyroid, blood sugar, etc.) with guidance

You will also support internal content and educational materials for clients.

Responsibilities

Client Coaching

Conduct weekly 1-on-1 coaching calls

Provide personalized fitness and lifestyle guidance (beginner ? advanced)

Support clients with habit-building, accountability, and consistency

Adapt training and lifestyle strategies based on chronic conditions (thyroid, diabetes, etc.)

Client Communication

Actively engage with clients via messaging/thread (daily/ongoing support)

Answer questions and provide guidance in a timely, professional manner

Track client progress and flag any concerns to the team

Content & Support Tasks

Create client-facing educational materials (Canva PDFs, guides, resources)

Assist in building a knowledge base for clients (nutrition, lifestyle, exercise)

Perform basic video editing (short-form/simple edits for content)

Follow company SOPs and protocols consistently

Requirements

Certified Personal Trainer (NASM, ACE, ISSA, or equivalent)

Strong understanding of:

Exercise programming (beginner to advanced)

Nutrition fundamentals

Lifestyle coaching (sleep, stress, habits)

Experience working with clients with chronic conditions (thyroid, diabetes, metabolic issues) is highly preferred

Excellent communication and coaching skills

Organized, detail-oriented, and able to multitask

Comfortable using tools like Canva and basic video editing software

Professional, reliable, and responsive

Training Provided

You will be trained in our functional medicine protocols and frameworks

Clear guidance on coaching expectations, communication standards, and client journey

Work Schedule

Part-time: 4 hours/day

1:00 PM – 5:00 PM PST M-F (fixed schedule)

Compensation

Competitive monthly pay (based on experience) $400 to start for 4hrs/day for 5 days a week. 

Opportunity for growth into a senior coach role as the company scales

Who This Is For

This role is ideal for someone who:

Wants to go beyond basic personal training into root-cause, holistic health

Enjoys working closely with clients and seeing real transformation

Is proactive, communicative, and growth-oriented

Wants to be part of a mission-driven health company

How to Apply

Please submit:

Your resume and certifications

Brief summary of your experience with clients (especially chronic conditions)

Any examples of coaching, programs, or content you’ve created

(Optional but preferred) A short Loom/video introducing yourself

If you’re passionate about helping people transform their health at a deeper level—not just workouts—we’d love to hear from you.





Part 1: Case Study (Advanced Coaching Scenario)

You are working with a client who is:

A high-level athlete

Recently diagnosed with Hashimoto’s disease

Experiencing:

Fatigue

Weight loss resistance

Fluctuating energy levels

Their goal:

Continue training at a high level (e.g., heavy compound lifts like 300 lb squats)

Improve body composition

Restore energy and long-term health



Part 2: Nutrition Strategy Question

Question:

What is the safest and most effective way to implement reverse dieting for long-term results?

Please include:

Your philosophy on reverse dieting

Step-by-step approach

Common mistakes to avoid

How you would adjust for someone with metabolic or thyroid dysfunction



For both parts, 2 paragraphs max.



Opportunity to grow, bonus and to onboard full-time.

title: Private Online Psychiatric Mental Health Tutor, Nurse practitioner, Doctor (Nonprofit initiative)
description:
Private Online Psychiatric Mental Health Tutor (Doctor or equivalent) 



Nonprofit Educational Initiative



About This Opportunity



This opportunity is part of a nonprofit educational mission focused on supporting learning and development in the field of mental health. The goal is to help equip individuals with strong foundational knowledge in psychiatric mental health, especially for future service, education, and mission driven work.



By participating, you will be contributing to a meaningful initiative that aims to expand understanding and access to mental health education.



Role Overview



We are looking for a licensed doctor, preferably a psychiatrist, or another highly qualified licensed mental health professional based in the Philippines to provide private one on one online tutoring in psychiatric mental health.



This is a paid education project. It is not a clinical role, not for patient care, not for independent practice, and not a substitute for formal school, certification, or licensure.



The purpose of this role is to deliver structured, high quality education in the core areas of psychiatric mental health knowledge.



About the Student



The student has a healthcare background, strong CDI and clinical documentation experience, and a strong ability to learn quickly. She already understands medical documentation and clinical workflows, but now wants to build deeper knowledge in psychiatric mental health.



She works full time, so the program must be:



• practical  

• organized  

• efficient  

• flexible  



What We Are Looking For



We are not looking for casual tutoring. We are looking for someone who can:



• mentor and guide the student in a structured way  

• design a clear learning pathway from start to finish  

• build a condensed, goal oriented program  

• help bridge clinical documentation knowledge into psychiatric thinking  



Your role is to equip and develop the student’s understanding in a serious and structured way, similar to a mentor or preceptor style relationship, but strictly for education only.



You should be able to take someone with a strong documentation background and help her understand how mental health professionals:



• assess patients  

• think through symptoms  

• form diagnoses  

• choose treatment approaches  

• understand medication logic  

• document psychiatric encounters  

• plan ongoing care  



Program Expectations



The program should be structured and intentional, not random sessions.



We are especially interested in applicants who can propose a clear and realistic learning plan explaining:



• how you would take the student from point A to point Z  

• how you would structure the learning, such as weekly sessions, modules, exercises, or guided case discussions  

• how you would pace the program for someone working full time  

• what outcomes the student should achieve by the end  



We expect approximately one session per week, although you may recommend the structure you believe is best.



Ideally, we hope the program can be taught in a boot camp style format, with the goal of bringing the student to a strong crash course level of psychiatric mental health knowledge in about 12 weeks or less, if realistic. We are open to your professional judgment on how best to structure this.



Core Topics to Cover



The program should build strong knowledge in areas such as:



• psychiatric assessment  

• psychiatric interviewing  

• mental status examination  

• DSM based diagnosis  

• differential diagnosis  

• psychopharmacology  

• medication selection and reasoning for learning purposes only  

• treatment planning  

• care planning  

• psychiatric documentation, including SOAP style notes  

• case based discussion  

• clinical reasoning  

• safety and risk assessment  

• crisis awareness and escalation thinking  

• basic psychotherapy concepts  

• patient education  

• follow up thinking and longitudinal care planning  



We are also especially interested in candidates who can teach the core foundation often associated with advanced psychiatric training, including the 3 Ps:



• advanced pathophysiology  

• advanced pharmacology  

• advanced health assessment  



The overall goal is to help the student build the type of psychiatric mental health knowledge commonly developed in PMHNP style training, while fully understanding that this tutoring program is for education only.



End Goal



By the end of the program, the student should have a strong educational foundation in how psychiatric mental health professionals think and approach:



• assessment  

• diagnosis  

• treatment options  

• medication reasoning  

• documentation  

• care planning  



This is about developing clinical thinking and understanding, not simply memorizing information.



Important Clarification



This program is strictly for education and knowledge building only.



It does not replace formal PMHNP education, certification, or licensure. The student is not seeking to practice based on this program alone.



Who This Is Best For



We are looking for:



• licensed doctor preferred  

• psychiatrist strongly preferred  

• other licensed mental health professionals with strong psychiatric experience may be considered  



Ideal candidates will be:



• experienced in psychiatric or mental health care  

• able to teach clearly in English  

• comfortable with one on one online teaching  

• organized, reliable, and professional  

• experienced in teaching, mentoring, tutoring, precepting, or curriculum building  

• able to design a structured learning program  



A practicing doctor in the Philippines who wants flexible remote teaching work may be a strong fit.



Program Setup



• fully remote and online  

• private one on one format  

• flexible schedule  

• approximately one session per week, or as proposed  

• short term, structured program preferred  

• Zoom based sessions are acceptable  



Budget



• total budget: PHP 35,000 for the full program  



Application Requirements



To apply, please send to:







• your CV or resume  

• your professional background  

• your license and credentials  

• your psychiatric or mental health experience  

• any teaching, tutoring, mentoring, or precepting experience  

• a short introduction video of 3 minutes or less introducing yourself, your experience, and your teaching style  

• a brief proposal explaining:  

?• how you would structure the program  

?• how you would take the student from a clinical documentation background to strong psychiatric mental health knowledge  

?• what your teaching approach would be  

?• what outcomes you expect by the end  

• your availability for an interview  



Final Note



This is a unique opportunity to combine professional expertise with meaningful educational impact. As part of this nonprofit initiative, your contribution helps support mental health education and future service in this field.



We are looking for someone who takes teaching seriously and is willing to guide the student with clarity, structure, and purpose.

- Good sense of color, print, and proportions

Preferred (Strong Advantage):

title: Operations Coordinator / Virtual Assistant (Remote, Part-Time)
description:
Role Overview

We are hiring a detail-oriented, proactive Operations Coordinator to provide remote support across two areas: (1) short-term rental (Airbnb) property operations, and (2) personal administrative and logistics support for a senior finance executive.



This is a part-time role (4 hours/day, 20 hours/week). The ideal candidate operates with minimal supervision, follows documented processes precisely, proactively identifies and resolves gaps, and communicates clearly and concisely. You will not be micromanaged — you will be trusted to execute.



Working Hours

• Eastern Time overlap is required, especially when communicating with housekeepers, vendors, or service providers in the US

• Flexibility to occasionally respond to urgent messages outside core hours is a plus



Part 1: Short-Term Rental Operations

Property Context

• Single Airbnb property with frequent guest turnovers

• 4–7 guest turnovers per month

• Guest messaging partially automated via Hospitable

• A property tablet is used for before/after photos, maintenance requests, and supply refill requests

• Photos sync to Google Photos; requests sync to Google Sheets

• Vendor communication handled through a centralized business number and



Responsibilities — Property Operations

• Confirm cleaner attendance before each scheduled checkout

• Follow up on missing before/after cleaning photos within 2 hours of expected turnover completion

• Review cleaning photos for quality control and flag any issues

• Monitor the Google Sheets supply request log and coordinate refills proactively

• Coordinate maintenance scheduling and basic vendor communication

• Gather 2–3 competitive quotes for non-urgent repairs (under $500 threshold)

• Escalate urgent property issues to the owner immediately

• Handle routine guest messaging not covered by Hospitable automation

• Sending weekly property status summaries every Monday



Part 2: Personal Admin & Logistics Support

Responsibilities — Admin Support

• Track deadlines, follow-ups, and open loops in a shared tracker

• Organize documents and folders in Google Drive per established structure

• Follow up with bills, autopay issues, service providers, lawyers, and administrative items

• Maintain a running open-loop tracker with owners and due dates

• Support travel and appointment logistics: research, shortlist, and summarize options

• Research and shortlist vendors or service providers with structured comparison summaries

• Draft follow-up
s and messages for owner review before sending

• Prepare agendas or briefing notes when requested



Required Skills & Qualities

• Proactive: surfaces solutions, not just problems

• Process-driven: comfortable with SOPs, checklists, and repeatable workflows

• Detail-oriented: catches gaps before they become issues

• Clear communicator: concise written English, responsive during ET hours

• Tech-comfortable: Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, Calendar), Hospitable, messaging tools

• Trustworthy and discreet: handles sensitive information with confidentiality

• Experienced in VA or remote operations roles (minimum 2 years preferred)

• Experience supporting property management or Airbnb operations is a strong plus



What We Offer

• Stable part-time role with clear expectations and documented systems

• Opportunity to grow into a broader operational support role

• Work from home with flexible start time within the ET morning window

• Supportive, low-drama environment with a clear principal who values your time

Compensation

Starting Rate: $600 – $700 USD/month (part-time, 20 hours/week)

? 90-Day Performance Review: After your first 90 days, we will conduct a formal review. Strong performers will receive a salary increase to $700 – $750/month. We reward consistency, proactivity, and ownership.

title: Video Editor – E-commerce Ads (Health Brand | Scandinavian Market)
description:
JOB TITLE

Video Editor – E-commerce Ads (Health Brand | Scandinavian Market)



JOB DESCRIPTION



Hey, thanks for reading this.

My name is Max. I run a health e-commerce brand. 



We sell health monitoring products across Sweden and Norway, and we're scaling fast into new markets.

I'm looking for a video editor who understands what it takes to make a paid ad actually perform, not just look good, but stop the scroll and convert.



What you'll actually be doing:



Editing short-form video ads for
 and Instagram, primarily 15 to 45 seconds

Taking raw product footage and TikTok-style clips and turning them into clean, high-converting ads

Adding subtitles, text overlays, transitions, and sound design that fits the European health market

Delivering multiple hook variations of the same ad so we can split test

Working at a consistent pace, we need a reliable output, not one great edit per month



What I need from you:



A portfolio or examples of e-commerce ads you've edited, specifically performance ads, not just brand films

You understand what makes someone stop scrolling, pacing, hooks, captions, sound

Experience with European or health-related brands is a strong plus

You're fast, organized, and communicate clearly in English

You can take feedback and implement it without ego



Tools:



Premiere Pro, CapCut, or similar, tell us what you use

Trello for task management



How to apply:

Send a short video in English, tell me about yourself, show me 2 to 3 ads you've edited, and explain what you did to make them perform. No examples, no consideration.



Looking forward to seeing your work.

— Max

title: Video Editor / Motion Graphics Editor for YouTube Business Documentary Channel
description:
Job Title:

Senior Video Editor / Motion Graphics Editor for YouTube Business Documentary Channel



Job Description:

Read this entire post carefully before applying.



To be considered, start your application with: PEPSI349



Applications without this word will be ignored immediately.



I am launching a serious YouTube channel called “Brand Misfire,” focused on high-quality business documentaries about corporate failures, brand collapses, and major marketing disasters.



I am not looking for a basic editor.

I am not looking for a clip assembler.

I am not looking for someone who only knows simple cuts, memes, gaming edits, or wedding edits.



I am looking for a highly capable long-form YouTube editor who understands:

- documentary storytelling

- cinematic pacing

- motion graphics

- clean infographic design

- strong sound design

- emotional build-up and tension



Reference Channel:

CoreSight YouTube Channel





Reference Videos:







Before applying, you must review the channel and both reference videos.



Only apply if you are genuinely confident that you can create a similar level of:

- pacing

- tension

- visual polish

- motion graphics quality

- storytelling atmosphere

- sound design intensity



If your portfolio does not clearly match this style, please do not apply.



This role requires someone who can take a script and voiceover and turn it into a polished documentary experience — not just a sequence of stock clips.



What I provide:

- fully researched English scripts

- professional AI voiceovers

- clear creative direction

- reference style and expectations

- ongoing work for the right editor



What you will do:

- edit 10–15 minute business documentary videos

- find strong archival / stock footage

- create cinematic motion graphics

- build clean infographic moments

- animate text, charts, numbers, and emphasis elements

- use sound design to create tension, impact, and momentum

- maintain audience retention through strong pacing and visual variation



Required Skills:

- strong long-form YouTube editing ability

- motion graphics experience (After Effects or equivalent)

- excellent sense of pacing and structure

- strong sound design instincts

- good English comprehension

- reliable communication

- ability to match a reference style very closely

- ability to work consistently on weekly deadlines



Budget:

- $100 USD per finished video

- 4 videos per month

- long-term work for the right person



Important:

This budget is fixed for now.

Do not apply if this rate does not work for you.



Paid Test:

I will shortlist only my favorite applicants.

The shortlisted editors will be paid $15 USD to edit the first 60 seconds of an upcoming video.

The editor with the best hook, pacing, visuals, and sound design will get the long-term role.



To apply, send exactly the following:

1. PEPSI349

2. 1 or 2 links only to your best documentary / motion graphics work

3. A short message explaining why you are a strong fit for this exact style

4. Confirmation that you accept:

   - $100 per video

   - the $15 paid test

5. The software you use for editing and motion graphics



Do not send:

- gaming edits

- wedding videos

- travel montages

- unrelated social media edits

- large portfolios with random samples



If you cannot match the reference style, do not apply.



I am looking for a serious long-term creative partner who understands documentary storytelling and high-retention editing.
